Between yesterday and today, I’ve flown across the country and back, chasing stories and gearing up to make OMG even stronger.

However, the future of my work and undercover investigative journalism relies on support from the public and Americans like you. I'm asking for your help to support me and my team in keeping up the pace and maintaining the investigations.

[**Can I count on you to make a donation today to help us cover our November expenses?**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e10223352/VaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUuYW5lZG90LmNvbS9va2VlZmVtZWRpYWdyb3VwL29tZy13ZS13b250LXN0b3AtbWItb20v/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTExMDY/LbmNseWdCUzlrVw/qP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9b21nLXdlLXdvbnQtc3RvcCZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPW9tJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj1tYiZ1dG1fdGVybT0yMTgzNV93cjEmYW1vdW50PTI1/gaQyu-w/sap414d4460)

Last month, we exposed a fraud on American taxpayers that costs us over $100 million – and that’s just from one organization. We have more in our sights.

The impact of our past investigations continues to resonate. In an unprecedented move, an officer with the State Department was **fired with the review and approval of the President** after we released our undercover investigation exposing the official's romantic relationship with a possible Chinese spy.

Just this week, we caught a non-profit executive instructing an illegal immigrant not just how to vote, **but WHO to vote for**.

And after today’s Election Day, we’ll have far more to report.

None of this is possible without the ongoing support of you and a handful of others who value truth and are willing to sacrifice to expose corruption.

[**Can I count on you to make a donation today?**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e10223352/VaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUuYW5lZG90LmNvbS9va2VlZmVtZWRpYWdyb3VwL29tZy13ZS13b250LXN0b3AtbWItb20v/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTExMDY/LbmNseWdCUzlrVw/qP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9b21nLXdlLXdvbnQtc3RvcCZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPW9tJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj1tYiZ1dG1fdGVybT0yMTgzNV93cjEmYW1vdW50PTI1/gaQyu-w/sap414d4460)

OMG’s expenses are growing as we continue to expand to keep up with the flood of tips and subsequent investigations. Every dollar makes a difference and helps fund our work to bring the truth to light.

In November, we're falling behind by $43,000, and I'm worried we won't be able to cover our expenses. [**Please, help me cover this gap by donating any amount you can today.**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e10223352/VaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUuYW5lZG90LmNvbS9va2VlZmVtZWRpYWdyb3VwL29tZy13ZS13b250LXN0b3AtbWItb20v/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTExMDY/LbmNseWdCUzlrVw/qP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9b21nLXdlLXdvbnQtc3RvcCZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPW9tJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj1tYiZ1dG1fdGVybT0yMTgzNV93cjEmYW1vdW50PTI1/gaQyu-w/sap414d4460)

Thank you so much for all that you do. As always, stay tuned for our subsequent investigation.

In Truth,
James O’Keefe

name: Jordan Zaslow
subject: I'm emotional to be writing this email
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Her Bold Move Action
body:
| | |
---
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118770751/580838629/1701071684?refcode=EM_F_251105ResultsB_JZ_10_NO_NG_N_LOGO&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQ1NWI2ZTE0LTFkYmItZjAxMS04MTk0LTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=FCmBQmjbGhCGZ73WFKCqseCzURAhZk0hgcs_KV9cTbg=)
---
Peter,

I am so deeply moved and, honestly, emotional to be writing this email.

Her Bold Move has been doing this work, supporting underserved women candidates since 2020. Last week, we sent you one of the most exciting emails we’ve ever sent, announcing our win rate for 2025 as it stood at the time: 97.5%.

As past General Election Nights taught us, “win rates” for orgs like ours tend to fall in November. After all, our candidates were up against incredibly well-funded, Trump-endorsed, anti-choice, often independently wealthy opponents – and they were often running for seats that had never been held by a woman before in history.

[**On Tuesday, nearly EVERY. SINGLE. ONE. of the candidates on the Her Bold Move roster (and we’ve added a few since the primaries) WON their races or received enough votes to qualify for a runoff.

**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118770755/580838636/1701071684?refcode=EM_F_251105ResultsB_JZ_10_NO_NG_N_L1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQ1NWI2ZTE0LTFkYmItZjAxMS04MTk0LTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=FCmBQmjbGhCGZ73WFKCqseCzURAhZk0hgcs_KV9cTbg=)**That means HER BOLD MOVE’s new win rate is 98%.**

We want to take a moment to level set: our candidates were up against incredibly challenging odds. These were not classic “blue” candidates in “blue” districts. Many of these candidates were underfunded, underserved, underresourced – classic underdogs. For some of these candidates, Her Bold Move was their only national endorsement. For others, HBM was the only support they could count on.

**Our win rate serves as proof that the programs we’ve built at Her Bold Move, programs which we’ve specifically designed to support _women_ candidates running in the _toughest races in the country,_ [**are working.**

Her Bold Move is successfully CHANGING THE OUTCOMES of elections in the parts of the country where women need representation MOST. We are just getting started, and we would be honored to have you join us in this work: We depend on supporters like you to make this work possible. You can help us continue to shatter glass ceilings by rushing $20.26 or more today. **](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118770758/580838637/1701071684?refcode=EM_F_251105ResultsB_JZ_10_NO_NG_N_L2&amount=20.26&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQ1NWI2ZTE0LTFkYmItZjAxMS04MTk0LTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=FCmBQmjbGhCGZ73WFKCqseCzURAhZk0hgcs_KV9cTbg=)

Our team is looking forward to sharing more information in the coming days with the details of our candidates’ victories. In the meantime, I’m sending an ENORMOUS congratulations to each and every one of our phenomenal candidates. It was our honor to support you.

The future is brighter with these women in the halls of power,

Jordan Zaslow
President & Founder
Her Bold Move

\----------- Forwarded message ---------
---
Peter,

Many of you have written to us that you feel a sense of despair when you think about the future of American politics. So much corruption. So much awful policy. Where are the “adults in the room” or the “leaders with integrity”....

But here is some much-needed GREAT and ENCOURAGING news: Over the past 12 months, Her Bold Move has supported around 40 candidates in primaries and special elections ACROSS AMERICA.

In 12 months, **only 1 candidate** from the entire Her Bold Move roster **has _lost_ a race.** That means we’ve won more than 97% of the races we’ve been involved with since Election Day 2024.

Here’s the thing: “Win rate” often comes with different implications – and it can be used to pressure orgs to support candidates who already had a high likelihood of winning based on fundraising numbers, incumbency advantage, or the political leaning of their district.

But at Her Bold Move, we see it as proof that our programs, **_specifically designed to support women candidates running in the _toughest_ _races_ in the country –_** are working.

[**Her Bold Move has repeatedly changed the outcomes of elections. We support candidates running for seats in majority male rooms and/or in states with abortion bans. In many cases, we’re our candidates’ only national endorsement – but we still help them beat the odds AND WIN! You can be a part of this uniquely impactful movement for women by rushing $20.25 RIGHT NOW. Let’s keep up this incredible momentum before Election Day 2025 – happening THIS TUESDAY!**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118770761/580838640/1701071684?refcode=EM_F_251105ResultsB_JZ_10_NO_NG_N_L3&amount=20.25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQ1NWI2ZTE0LTFkYmItZjAxMS04MTk0LTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=FCmBQmjbGhCGZ73WFKCqseCzURAhZk0hgcs_KV9cTbg=)

name: The blues
subject: I don’t want Lauren Boebert dead
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado
body:
| | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001trhCZCgXSn83DrZKdcJ0gDKAOSbZuuFv_n45TE_0LMAPyH-zv7XRmG-DvW9X0xpWjB1vdnv290eYrUxG_TE6AI8F32SCtxKLQO2ftXOpUqFj-XvflstZYyvcm-w0pDDETDwhVr4AgQXn_NzMPyhVBcv8GDdK4spgrX45OSPraiTcJH54SB2maRYPMeRWqvmGLS7tgYvjsJ9hwrUCSlHCjc_ioVp43e2oJaSl_STBq18=&c=KnY8fQoJ6sByOdlPCetWrHp7KamDDi5k2m94Wv0tfJKxEXo_viZ-WA==&ch=6xU8wliee74xtqhgOPrPsQeEPl33V1pjPcpf1XAxElO4T4shIWMsog==)
---
|

Peter,
On Election day, rather than helping her embattled GOP colleagues who got blown out across the country, Lauren Boebert was doomscrolling on Twitter.
Among the things she posted was a tweet agreeing with a GOP operative who said that the left wants them dead.
---
| [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001trhCZCgXSn83DrZKdcJ0gDKAOSbZuuFv_n45TE_0LMAPyH-zv7XRmG-DvW9X0xpWjB1vdnv290eYrUxG_TE6AI8F32SCtxKLQO2ftXOpUqFj-XvflstZYyvcm-w0pDDETDwhVr4AgQXn_NzMPyhVBcv8GDdK4spgrX45OSPraiTcJH54SB2maRYPMeRWqvmGLS7tgYvjsJ9hwrUCSlHCjdSbRCBglwgC25Ia1PiC0qs=&c=KnY8fQoJ6sByOdlPCetWrHp7KamDDi5k2m94Wv0tfJKxEXo_viZ-WA==&ch=6xU8wliee74xtqhgOPrPsQeEPl33V1pjPcpf1XAxElO4T4shIWMsog==)
---

I don't want Lauren Boebert dead. I don't want anyone on the right dead.
I disagree with her on fundamentally every issue but I believe that she has a right to believe in what she believes. Now, I believe our ideas are better and I will fight to ensure ours win out over hers but we will accomplish this via political means not through violence.
Boebert, on the other hand, sees violence as the key to attaining her aims. She helped orchestrate January 6th. She's called for the creation of a "holy army" to wipe out the left in Colorado. And calls Democrats Reptiles and Jihadis, and Trans people demons.
I'd say she is the pot calling the kettle black, but no one on the left is calling for violence. Boebert has invented a fake narrative that we want violence and people dead so that she can in turn justify violence against us.
As a student of philosopher Thich Nhat Hanh, I believe wholeheartedly that spiraling American political violence calls for radical peace.
Stopping the epidemic of political violence in this country starts with unseating Lauren Boebert. She is in Congress because of just 546 votes and when I challenged her in 2024 I had the best performance a Democrat has ever had in this district. Now I'm back to finish the job.
[Please can you make a monthly donation of $50, $25, or whatever amount is meaningful to you, to help us continue to rapidly scale our campaign to beat Boebert, flip Colorado's 4th, and take back the House for Democrats?](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001trhCZCgXSn83DrZKdcJ0gDKAOSbZuuFv_n45TE_0LMAPyH-zv7XRmG-DvW9X0xpWMF95gcj6qpxFu_u4oQNzrGqHiF22rHwGm84Am157guLVfeBu2Y6_LOm1qvWrKHjtq24z3dgCOXOn985ZsPzxxTxnMMkR-sujkZrn642AeFrGcdjjixcuPwQF3WejCj3IsOduxBeQBxPb8R_49IDghQypqHA2H-21ckG60YDRkboWcWwtabZa7Q==&c=KnY8fQoJ6sByOdlPCetWrHp7KamDDi5k2m94Wv0tfJKxEXo_viZ-WA==&ch=6xU8wliee74xtqhgOPrPsQeEPl33V1pjPcpf1XAxElO4T4shIWMsog==)

clean_body: | | | | | == | Peter, On Election day, rather than helping her embattled GOP colleagues who got blown out across the country, Lauren Boebert was doomscrolling on Twitter. Among the things she posted was a tweet agreeing with a GOP operative who said that the left wants them dead. | == I don't want Lauren Boebert dead. I don't want anyone on the right dead. I disagree with her on fundamentally every issue but I believe that she has a right to believe in what she believes. Now, I believe our ideas are better and I will fight to ensure ours win out over hers but we will accomplish this via political means not through violence. Boebert, on the other hand, sees violence as the key to attaining her aims. She helped orchestrate January 6th. She's called for the creation of a "holy army" to wipe out the left in Colorado. And calls Democrats Reptiles and Jihadis, and Trans people demons. I'd say she is the pot calling the kettle black, but no one on the left is calling for violence. Boebert has invented a fake narrative that we want violence and people dead so that she can in turn justify violence against us. As a student of philosopher Thich Nhat Hanh, I believe wholeheartedly that spiraling American political violence calls for radical peace. Stopping the epidemic of political violence in this country starts with unseating Lauren Boebert. She is in Congress because of just 546 votes and when I challenged her in 2024 I had the best performance a Democrat has ever had in this district. Now I'm back to finish the job. == Please use the links in this email to start a monthly donation through ActBlue: | | | == | == | == | | | == | == | == | Thank you for standing with us during these trying times. In solidarity, Trisha Calvarese Democrat for CO-04 | | | | == | Mail checks to: Trisha 4 CO 9609 S University Blvd., PO Box 631322 Highlands Ranch, CO 80163 | Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado Trisha Calvarese for Congress | 9609 S University Blvd. PO Box 631322 | Highlands Ranch, CO 80163 US | | | | | | |

name: Jason Miyares
subject: It has been the honor of my lifetime
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Miyares for Virginia |
body:
Thank you for the privilege of serving as your People's Protector.| | | [](http://links.jasonforvirginia.com/s/c/qhTOGH8PZwx9_6KQVYa0nUzHijP4mrVqqi1eQ7H0_fgFbcRxlI-2lzZjslix5v8Sh83EQPaXcHWiDIgK0ocL0FBzKju7MP3_4aUiIWj0LMdRttQCk2Nb4X5iNESb91D6_bQssOwUI1Hd-XDsf-lr5Q5QG8nAin-RV6Ib3M_Mmyy_UaNPWn1i7dpeHe-Pf_OZh-bQ_Fda1sSuGVoBGdLZBb09NsnitOOSdcWHetQWTxLBlQZ6xoqTQFTDB2BhWU2GcE2KSurf4PBZvqImGbrsxte6DM8ASL0TEBip1qmtZ0whLhIvj7V5vARwDH4gIZWW8BBp-00KurZlMEBvfigTQrh6WzSCgPW1bQ/hc3E7E7tIkwLktF7mmyufN6wpQQ0x3js/14)
---
Friend, This race has been decided, and while we held our ground, we came up short. I could never have come this far without your steadfast support, friend. **It has been the honor of my life to serve as your 48th Attorney General.** When we started this campaign, we knew it would be tough. We were fighting the tides of history, but we fought because Virginia's safety has always been my mission — and my reverence for the rule of law is deeply personal. **Only in America** could the son of a Cuban refugee become a state delegate, then rise to become the highest-ranking Latino official and Attorney General in Virginia's history. I carry with me my family's story from Cuba and the lessons my Uncle Ángel taught me about what it means to live in a nation of laws, not of men. Some of my proudest moments were welcoming new Americans at naturalization ceremonies, remembering my own mother asking me to teach her our Pledge of Allegiance before she took her oath. **Sixty years ago, a 19-year-old girl, scared and alone, boarded a plane with nothing but hope.** Mamita, thank you for always being my North Star. The people of Virginia have spoken, and I respect their choice. **I wish Jay Jones the very best in this new role.** Considering the past six weeks, I know some of you will find this hard to accept. But we need an Attorney General who wakes up every day focused on keeping Virginians safe, and it is my sincere hope that he does just that. From the outset, we made public safety our top priority. **We cut murders by 30% with the help of Operation Ceasefire,** drove the largest drop in addiction deaths in the United States, took enough fentanyl off our streets to save millions of lives, and never wavered in our support for the men and women in uniform. I want to thank my wife Paige, my three beautiful daughters, and my mom Miriam for their love and sacrifice throughout this journey. To all of my team, staff, allies, and supporters like you — I'm truly blessed to have you by my side. While we didn't win this week, I'm proud of everything we achieved together. **Republicans in Virginia — we are down, not out. Elections are pendulums. Tonight, it swung against our party. It will swing back, it always does.** Until my last day in office, ensuring your family's safety will remain my highest priority. I don't yet know what my next chapter holds, but I know that **my story isn't over.** This isn't goodbye — it's see you later. I will always fight for Virginia, no matter what role I serve in. **Thank you for always believing in me, friend.** Thank you for proving that principled leadership can make a real difference. **Please join me in praying for Jay Jones' wisdom and guidance as he takes on this sacred responsibility. And always remember, we are fellow Americans before we are political opponents.** With eternal gratitude,
---

body:
Your name is still MISSING from Marsha Blackburn’s final tally.

| | | | **Petition ID: JV839 - Peter**

**Attempt 1: FAILED TO RESPOND**
**Attempt 2: FAILED TO RESPOND**

**Final Attempt: RESPONSE PENDING**

_Peter, I've emailed you TWICE. And I know my team has sent you at least 3 texts about this!_

At the beginning of the year, I began collecting signatures on my petition to enforce voter ID laws in all 50 states. Conservatives across the country stepped up and signed their names to protect our democracy.

But, if I don't receive another 18 signatures before 11:59 PM, I’m afraid we won't have the grassroots support necessary to protect our democracy and save the integrity of our elections from the Democrats' electioneering schemes once and for all.

**And your name is still MISSING from my final tally.**

Peter, four other Republicans in your zip code have already signed on to secure our elections.

[So, will you please join them and add your name now before time runs out and I pull my final count?](https://trk.emails.marshablackburn.)[HELP SECURE OUR ELECTIONS

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

We encourage you to thoroughly examine this mеssagе from our associates, as it carries substantial benefits for уоu. | | | 2025: Retirement Age Will Rise—Are You Ready? | | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. If they’re raising the retirement age again , do you really think your benefits are safe? | | | 🟡 News From "American Alternative Assets" | (Sponsored) ---|--- Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from YourCapitalMinds.com, an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. name: Elizabeth Nader
subject: We have our work cut out for us
body:
Peter, We have a new Governor-elect. And we are under no illusions about the Lunacy to come. Today, my heart is heavy for our state and the future. I’m not going to mince words. This outcome nudges New Jersey
| | [ ]()
---
Peter, We have a new Governor-elect. And we are under no illusions about the Lunacy to come. Today, my heart is heavy for our state and the future. I’m not going to mince words. This outcome nudges New Jersey even closer to the precipice. And while Governor-elect Sherrill may have played it safe with her campaign trail rhetoric, we know her true colors (and the radical special interests that have her in their pockets). Now that she’s been elected, the cloak of “moderation” will be gone and her radical views will be out in full blaze, now with the power to do damage statewide.
* Indoctrination of our children.
* High taxes.
* Stifling, heavy-handed government.
* Soft on crime.
* Rampant wokeism.

But I also hold on to hope. We must. If we, the Common Sense Majority, abandon our state and go dark, then hope is truly lost. But I don’t believe we will. So, Common Sense Majority, get ready to be loud, to call out the BS, and to provide needed accountability. If Governor-elect Sherrill thinks this is a coronation and the state will fall gamely in line, she’s got another thing coming. If we do our jobs, Governor-elect Sherrill will hear the voice of the Common Sense Majority unceasingly for the next four years. And when voters she misled discover who she really is, they’ll need a place to go. This will be that place. Thank you for remaining a steadfast and common sense voice despite the political madness and Leftist Lunacy around us. Because of you, I’m hopeful. For New Jersey, Elizabeth Nader
---
| [ ]()
---
President
Common Sense Club
---
|
---

name: John Fetterman
subject: how we can support our neighbors right now
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fetterman for PA
body:
[ https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/51612?t=1001&akid=10410%2E434096%2EULw1Au ]John Fetterman

Hi Derek,

SNAP funding has run dry – and families are going hungry right now. If you want to do something about it, donate to a local food bank today.

https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/52285?t=1002&akid=10410%2E434096%2EULw1Au

Here’s how we got here:

First, Trump and his crew pushed through their Big, Ugly Bill – the one with the largest cuts to SNAP in history. They gutted food assistance for working families, veterans, and kids.

Then, when the government shut down, Democrats tried to pass an emergency bill to keep food programs funded. Republicans blocked it – and left town.

Now, there are SNAP emergency funds that a judge ruled must be released immediately. Trump’s response? Pay half, maybe, sometime in the next few months. Meanwhile, kids are hungry.

We should NOT be playing political games with food stamps while working families, veterans, and kids pay the price.

America’s better than this. PA sure as hell is.

Join me in showing it. Chip in to your local food bank right now to support our neighbors and help make sure PA families can eat – because as long as Trump and his crew won’t lift a finger to help, it's on us to look out for each other.

John ❤️

clean_body: [ ]John Fetterman Hi Derek, SNAP funding has run dry – and families are going hungry right now. If you want to do something about it, donate to a local food bank today. Here’s how we got here: First, Trump and his crew pushed through their Big, Ugly Bill – the one with the largest cuts to SNAP in history. They gutted food assistance for working families, veterans, and kids. Then, when the government shut down, Democrats tried to pass an emergency bill to keep food programs funded. Republicans blocked it – and left town. Now, there are SNAP emergency funds that a judge ruled must be released immediately. Trump’s response? Pay half, maybe, sometime in the next few months. Meanwhile, kids are hungry. We should NOT be playing political games with food stamps while working families, veterans, and kids pay the price. America’s better than this. PA sure as hell is. Join me in showing it. Chip in to your local food bank right now to support our neighbors and help make sure PA families can eat – because as long as Trump and his crew won’t lift a finger to help, it's on us to look out for each other. John ❤️

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

**Washington’s new GENIUS Act is quietly preparing to cancel your cash and replace it with programmable, trackable money.**

The GENIUS Act is already reshaping the American financial system behind closed doors.
[ ](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=b9c9e081330b43fd9ffc864a15a77a4c&_e=fFp0ebPK2WpJHXgAge6a0KktMpkOMMa9PqJmxwfK8pbCSDDJM322BeEch33H9lmE5rS-Gp9mJkPo4ij4FeYfqUAnwlPiUwH_1dk_m5nPhLCg4vxjztIW9uKo8PfjXtjhJy-4WYUPOyKntKOef-0tzCQRfuIwp9Heukl-jqA3Mok0g1Y_oL2Grnl-QCnGVJC_K5c2cFo0ojmNYEQ0ej8iVg__jFOg0PWJ9AQWkM9kwQdPUBulPPDykU65NgkiTSBTogIxsDLBH-8itWKxyVqrPMXfYLEqYtJu28EOD_ISIWmCIpamSaV6CHSLZECTNwmUgewF33rvL2LCrWboOJCHjh5qrghHkqSrbZanG-ape3xqM_kH6a5I-xJd8hgNLcfQ29200A-PPAS1gI2QMb4k-Ba8AGSRJ2KbE_4SOwQd1oXHWmNIfZ-jYFzRCHGq-dymZVDI96ZoW7fgetR5hlWZFdD6gLC7ILx4kT0kiwXIN31j-oqlObg4c-3cN2yMQA9naEhrXI3y1BaiUIsfkyqgQem7pmeQvWZM9TmA7dnbaw8yVyr-Ly8KgCKs9SUiOPZP)
This federal law hands private tech giants and Wall Street insiders the power to issue digital dollars backed by Washington but controlled outside your vote.

Once embedded into banks, payroll services, and retail networks, these programmable dollars will track, trace, and restrict every transaction you make.

**You won’t be able to opt out — because once your paycheck, your debit card, and your favorite apps are wired to the system, escape becomes impossible.**

This is the moment to act. A free guide reveals three urgent strategies you can put in place today so “they” can’t dictate when, where, or how you spend your own money tomorrow.

[Click here now before the switch flips and it’s too late.](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=b9c9e081330b43fd9ffc864a15a77a4c&_e=fFp0ebPK2WpJHXgAge6a0KktMpkOMMa9PqJmxwfK8pbCSDDJM322BeEch33H9lmE5rS-Gp9mJkPo4ij4FeYfqUAnwlPiUwH_1dk_m5nPhLCg4vxjztIW9uKo8PfjXtjhJy-4WYUPOyKntKOef-0tzCQRfuIwp9Heukl-jqA3Mok0g1Y_oL2Grnl-QCnGVJC_nSynTN8a9Mix0PLQuhKXEBJogGxlZUxgEFW9oEx47oYCqekihdapupaLmRQCy_ZqSLanfaYYyvbwyw9Rt_q65fEccJJz3-WkRi6iq6UQrPwJf6ZnVfyW38382CnnBfJA7WgHH_oy82XUUhVnZ0EmFBUR7jBQbHHsl21jQtFsaMu8bHQ_j5lDHz2AMR0YQP7VClOPUjVeVn_sHc6bSNlFlsJSQEcMuufl0_qHKwlkG0a0Rn6tNaa0V443QFUjCRU8tkRnMSa1i9yCKKAAfaw77Ki-yRZkXdney29ubjDOj21B-JR6zctmdPDxGD_vRBFlqkkT7O13dGBIktcE6kze2rbl93DnfA-aa3i7E6g17ZP-DZQY3StjnbsojtOpcd9l)

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

We encourage you to thoroughly examine this mеssagе from our associates, as it carries substantial benefits for уоu. | | | Act Now: 3 Strategies to Shield Your Savings Before the Switch | | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. The GENIUS Act is already reshaping the American financial system behind closed doors. This federal law hands private tech giants and Wall Street insiders the power to issue digital dollars backed by Washington but controlled outside your vote. Once embedded into banks, payroll services, and retail networks, these programmable dollars will track, trace, and restrict every transaction you make. You won’t be able to opt out — because once your paycheck, your debit card, and your favorite apps are wired to the system, escape becomes impossible. This is the moment to act. A free guide reveals three urgent strategies you can put in place today so “they” can’t dictate when, where, or how you spend your own money tomorrow. | | | 🟡 News From "American Alternative Assets" | (Sponsored) ---|--- Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from YourCapitalMinds.com, an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. name: Adam Schiff
subject: After Tuesday’s election, the message is clear:
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY SCHIFF FOR SENATE
body:
|
# [](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/61?t=1&akid=9157%2E5257348%2ESln5kt)
Derek —
Tuesday was Election Day, and Democrats notched big victories in California, Virginia, New Jersey, Pennsylvania, and beyond.
In my home state, we passed Prop. 50 by an overwhelming majority of the vote, which will help us try to even the playing field with Republicans who are redrawing congressional maps in Texas and other states. _And fight fire with fire._
Californians across the state made it clear that they will not stand by as Donald Trump tries to rig the next election before it begins.
This was a hugely successful night for Democrats, for voters, and for our democracy, and the message is clear:
**Voters are tired of Trump and Republicans, they are ready for someone who will work tirelessly on affordability, and they want Democrats to fight relentlessly.**
**[Will you help keep up this momentum running all the way through the 2026 midterm elections, where we will have the opportunity to flip the House, the Senate, and governor’s races across the country? Pitch in $5, $15, or whatever you can afford now.](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1014?c0=q42025_abs_em.dd-KUe&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20251106_AM1_V1_S1&t=4&akid=9157%2E5257348%2ESln5kt)**

We worked hard leading up to these 2025 elections, and we should take a moment to celebrate our victories.
But we also can’t lose sight of the fact that we have less than a year until the midterm elections. And already, Trump is attacking voting rights on Truth Social, casting doubt on election security and election results.
It will take a lot of hard work to win again — but I know we can do it.
Thanks for being with us.

Since Inauguration Day in January, there's been a more than 1,000% increase in assaults against our ICE officers.

| | | | **
**Hey Friend, Vice President JD Vance here.
Since Inauguration Day in January, there's been a **more than 1,000% increase** in assaults against our ICE officers.

No one has done more to encourage this dangerous lawlessness than Democratic politicians, who simply refuse to stop demonizing these brave men and women putting it all on the line to keep us safe.

**Well, what if I told you we're on the verge of electing yet another radical Left Democrat so brazen that she actually filmed herself laughing and bragging about "bullying" – _her words, not mine_ – ICE and law enforcement?**

**If the patriots who helped deliver our 2024 landslide victory do not engage in a major way these next few weeks, that's exactly what may happen in Tennessee on _December 2nd_ , where the whole nation will be watching a critical House special election.**

Not next year. __This year.__

I don't have to tell you how razor-thin our margin is in the House, and losing just one seat like this wouldn't just spell trouble for next year. We would feel the pain immediately.

Not long ago, Dr. Shafir Botner, director of MAGEN DAVID ADOM’s paramedic school and a Medevac helicopter paramedic, was treating injured civilians at the scene of a rocket strike in Be’er Sheva, Israel, when he looked up and recognized a familiar face. There, working alongside the MAGEN DAVID ADOM team to assist the injured civilians was Chief Inspector Or Yosef of the Israel Police.

The last time Dr. Botner had seen him, Inspector Yosef was fighting for his life after being critically wounded by an RPG while helping civilians during the Nova Music Festival massacre on October 7, 2023.

A MAGEN DAVID ADOM paramedic was treating the inspector when he realized Yosef would not survive long enough to make it to the hospital in his ambulance. So, he immediately called for a helicopter.

When Dr. Botner and his crew landed, they quickly loaded Inspector Yosef into the helicopter.
They knew every second mattered
https://track.americanpatriotdaily.com/?xtl=t0sh775makoujfhr96tftiup19zjsr5gp7qoo89nnirzbecmwh5nv2864of49nlgvon00qya16j3oms9dl7z8xjb2b6tkbiglqzzf92lp463joplpzs0m2ss1tz3olzqp7f5b2rtfvdqup8wg3759obe1mvlkyt7t8njl&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t5b9jk&__onlt=t
.

“He was in very serious condition and had lost a massive amount of blood.

I did everything I could for him.”

But now, this patient who so nearly died on October 7 was not only still alive, he was also outside helping others. Seeing Inspector Yosef up on his feet and back on the job filled Dr. Botner with emotion.

“Or Yosef is truly an inspiration. To return to the field after such a devastating injury and help others injured in a missile attack — that’s extraordinary.”

I wish you could go with me sometime when I visit the MAGEN DAVID ADOM National Dispatch Center just outside Tel Aviv or one of the 206 MAGEN DAVID ADOM emergency medical stations located across Israel. There you will encounter some of the most extraordinary men and women you may ever meet.

There is something about the determination and compassion I see in their faces that always moves me. They are there to save lives.

HELP SAVE LIVES IN ISRAEL ►
https://track.americanpatriotdaily.com/?xtl=t0sh775makoujfhr96tftiup19zjsr5gp7qoo89nnirzbecmwh5nv2864of49nlgvon00qya16j3oms9dl7z8xjb2b6tkbiglqzzf92lp463joplpzs0m2ss1tz3olzqp7f5b2rtfvdqup8wg3759obe1mvlkyt7t8njl&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t5b9jk&__onlt=t

It takes grit and guts to do what they do every day. When a call comes in, they respond without hesitation — missiles may be falling, active shooters may be on the scene, buildings may be burning, but they are committed to going above and beyond to save as many lives as possible, even if it means putting their own lives on the line.

Again and again, these paramedics, EMTs, and first responders rise above fear, push beyond limits, and stand in the gap. Their bravery, their sacrifice, their relentless dedication — it’s nothing less than extraordinary.

May I ask you today to
give to support these exceptional women and men
https://track.americanpatriotdaily.com/?xtl=t0sh775makoujfhr96tftiup19zjsr5gp7qoo89nnirzbecmwh5nv2864of49nlgvon00qya16j3oms9dl7z8xjb2b6tkbiglqzzf92lp463joplpzs0m2ss1tz3olzqp7f5b2rtfvdqup8wg3759obe1mvlkyt7t8njl&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t5b9jk&__onlt=t
?

MAGEN DAVID ADOM relies almost entirely upon private donations and not government funding — which means the people of Israel are counting on the faithful financial support of generous friends like you so that help is able to come in moments of dire need.

Your gift
https://track.americanpatriotdaily.com/?xtl=t0sh775makoujfhr96tftiup19zjsr5gp7qoo89nnirzbecmwh5nv2864of49nlgvon00qya16j3oms9dl7z8xjb2b6tkbiglqzzf92lp463joplpzs0m2ss1tz3olzqp7f5b2rtfvdqup8wg3759obe1mvlkyt7t8njl&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t5b9jk&__onlt=t
will provide the medical supplies and equipment, blood reserves, and emergency vehicles that these extraordinary modern-day heroes must have to save every man, woman, and child possible.

God bless you,

Catherine Reed

Chief Executive Officer

GIVE TO SAVE LIVES ►
https://track.americanpatriotdaily.com/?xtl=t0sh775makoujfhr96tftiup19zjsr5gp7qoo89nnirzbecmwh5nv2864of49nlgvon00qya16j3oms9dl7z8xjb2b6tkbiglqzzf92lp463joplpzs0m2ss1tz3olzqp7f5b2rtfvdqup8wg3759obe1mvlkyt7t8njl&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t5b9jk&__onlt=t

MAGEN DAVID ADOM is Israel’s emergency services system, providing critical medical care and ambulance services for victims of terror, natural disasters, automobile accidents, and all other emergencies in Israel. It is also responsible for Israel’s national blood supply. MAGEN DAVID ADOM saves lives in Israel every day. Become a
Lifeline Partner
https://track.americanpatriotdaily.com/?xtl=1bnpvbp0dtrfemzcjsnzcequ75t8iduzwx71swilzghfrodj2p1bhn24h78xi9e82q1wwzoi6hy2apguvi3g9luyfaav49sjfit0mtiexs72wx5fu95vkuqiee84rwmlj7xcmauu9nz2sa99qr6b8o00ie0vwb65m&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t5b9jk&__onlt=t
now.

name: Team DNC
subject: Numbers don’t lie.
body:
---
Derek, with your help, Democrats were able to win big in the 2025 elections — delivering a Democratic resurgence. A Republican reckoning. But our work isn’t over, and we can’t let this momentum slow. We need to run right through the tape to take back Congress in 2026. Here’s the thing: Numbers don’t lie. We won these elections because Democrats volunteered, joined canvasses and phonebanks. You gave your time because you knew the outcomes of these elections _mattered._ Today, we’re asking you to join the Democrats who have already chipped in to help us keep our momentum going into the midterms by [**making your first donation of 2025.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-november-2025?amount=7&refcode=em_20251106_5184_fr&refcode2=5184_22930333_kT3IMl&akid=5184%2E22930333%2EkT3IMl) Last night’s wins are just the beginning. To take back Congress, we need to continue to mobilize people to volunteer, build the resources we’ll need to invest in every state, and ultimately get people to the polls. That work is impossible without the help of our most committed supporters.

[**Derek, the most impactful thing you can do right now to help us reach the voters we need to win in 2026, 2028, and beyond is to give $7 to the DNC. Use this link to do just that.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-november-2025?amount=7&refcode=em_20251106_5184_fr&refcode2=5184_22930333_kT3IMl&akid=5184%2E22930333%2EkT3IMl) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._ | | | **[Donate $7](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-november-2025?express_lane=true&amount=7&refcode=em_20251106_5184_fr&refcode2=5184_22930333_kT3IMl&akid=5184%2E22930333%2EkT3IMl)**

name: Tyler Kalogeros-Treschuk
subject: $9.7 million in destruction
body:
A global attack on reproductive freedom
Dear Peter,

Last month, Trump "proudly" rejoined a global anti-abortion and anti-LGBTQ+ declaration.

[**Trump's actions reflect alignment with a growing movement that hides behind "pro-family" language while attacking reproductive freedom and access to care.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/118780798/580869271/-415929393?sourceid=1150941&utm_campaign=26emkge-1102aax-on&utm_medium=ea&utm_source=email&utm_content=contraceptives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9DRlJSL0NGUlIvMS85NTk4MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkYmU1ZWIxZC0yOGJiLWYwMTEtODE5NC02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=GjLrLJmcIOJZHYmAeBUPDfovRjPAN5QrGlW8EO2sE2s=&emci=22cb277e-91ba-f011-8e61-00224824e08d&emdi=dbe5eb1d-28bb-f011-8194-6045bda9d96b&ceid=954237)

The Trump administration's plan to destroy $9.7 million worth of contraceptives intended for women in low-income countries reflects this same ideology—prioritizing politics over the health and rights of women and girls around the world.

[**Tell your representatives that this is more than a bureaucratic failure—it's a preventable humanitarian crisis!**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/118780799/580869272/-415929393?sourceid=1150941&utm_campaign=26emkge-1102aax-on&utm_medium=ea&utm_source=email&utm_content=contraceptives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9DRlJSL0NGUlIvMS85NTk4MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkYmU1ZWIxZC0yOGJiLWYwMTEtODE5NC02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=GjLrLJmcIOJZHYmAeBUPDfovRjPAN5QrGlW8EO2sE2s=&emci=22cb277e-91ba-f011-8e61-00224824e08d&emdi=dbe5eb1d-28bb-f011-8194-6045bda9d96b&ceid=954237)

In solidarity,

Tyler Kalogeros-Treschuk
Senior Director, Individual Giving

clean_body: A global attack on reproductive freedom Dear Peter, Last month, Trump "proudly" rejoined a global anti-abortion and anti-LGBTQ+ declaration. The Trump administration's plan to destroy $9.7 million worth of contraceptives intended for women in low-income countries reflects this same ideology—prioritizing politics over the health and rights of women and girls around the world. name: Team Bell
subject: No one should have to worry about putting food on the table. End of story.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BELL FOR MISSOURI
body:
Here are some helpful resources for St. Louis families: _If you or someone you know is struggling right now to keep food on the table, there are places ready to help, including local food pantries and meal programs that you can find[**here.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118782031/580873409/728264712?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDYzODkiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiM2U1YmViYTItMjliYi1mMDExLTgxOTQtNjA0NWJkYTlkOTZiIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Ekwyp57d8BFtH_nv7IRx67TQqFzzO622h1XIu9AWgr0=&emci=aec1c541-7bba-f011-8e61-00224824e08d&emdi=3e5beba2-29bb-f011-8194-6045bda9d96b&ceid=1020964)_ Peter,

This shutdown is already painful enough for the American people, and hunger should not be an added consequence. But Trump is playing games with people's lives, and after too much bureaucratic nonsense, Americans enrolled in the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) are receiving delayed and _reduced_ benefits this month.

**To make matters worse,** the United States Department of Agriculture just told grocery stores they aren't allowed to offer special discounts to SNAP recipients during the shutdown. Unless stores have a special waiver, all customers must be charged the full price, _even in businesses that_ want _to help families._

SNAP recipients are our friends, family, and neighbors, and Wesley is working to make sure St. Louisans are getting the assistance they need as soon as possible.

The Trump administration's blatant attempt to demonize and "other" SNAP recipients is nothing short of ghoulish. Our city is full of people who look out for each other. It's time to stand up for our neighbors to ensure that everyone has the support they need.

[**If you or someone you know is struggling right now to keep food on the table, there are places ready to help, including local food pantries and meal programs that you can find here.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118782039/580873437/728264712?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDYzODkiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiM2U1YmViYTItMjliYi1mMDExLTgxOTQtNjA0NWJkYTlkOTZiIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Ekwyp57d8BFtH_nv7IRx67TQqFzzO622h1XIu9AWgr0=&emci=aec1c541-7bba-f011-8e61-00224824e08d&emdi=3e5beba2-29bb-f011-8194-6045bda9d96b&ceid=1020964)

[**If you're able, please consider making a donation to Operation Food Search, a local organization here in Missouri working every day to make sure no one in our community goes hungry.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118782045/580873470/-1317988266?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDYzODkiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiM2U1YmViYTItMjliYi1mMDExLTgxOTQtNjA0NWJkYTlkOTZiIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Ekwyp57d8BFtH_nv7IRx67TQqFzzO622h1XIu9AWgr0=&emci=aec1c541-7bba-f011-8e61-00224824e08d&emdi=3e5beba2-29bb-f011-8194-6045bda9d96b&ceid=1020964)

clean_body: Here are some helpful resources for St. Louis families: If you or someone you know is struggling right now to keep food on the table, there are places ready to help, including local food pantries and meal programs that you can find Peter, This shutdown is already painful enough for the American people, and hunger should not be an added consequence. But Trump is playing games with people's lives, and after too much bureaucratic nonsense, Americans enrolled in the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) are receiving delayed and reduced benefits this month. To make matters worse, the United States Department of Agriculture just told grocery stores they aren't allowed to offer special discounts to SNAP recipients during the shutdown. Unless stores have a special waiver, all customers must be charged the full price, even in businesses that want to help families. SNAP recipients are our friends, family, and neighbors, and Wesley is working to make sure St. Louisans are getting the assistance they need as soon as possible. The Trump administration's blatant attempt to demonize and "other" SNAP recipients is nothing short of ghoulish. Our city is full of people who look out for each other. It's time to stand up for our neighbors to ensure that everyone has the support they need. Thank you, Team Bell | PAID FOR BY BELL FOR MISSOURI PO Box 190669, ST LOUIS, MO 63119
