* * *
**[Together with American Hartford Gold](https://substack.com/redirect/5fbb4a0a-b60c-434f-9eec-91c3d2042067?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
# **[Trump's Reset Can Give Birth To America's Greatest Era Yet](https://substack.com/redirect/5fbb4a0a-b60c-434f-9eec-91c3d2042067?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/5fbb4a0a-b60c-434f-9eec-91c3d2042067?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**In 1933, Executive Order 6102 forced everyday Americans to hand over their gold at a fixed rate.** Everyday citizens lost a sizable amount of their hard earned wealth at the stroke of FDR's pen.
**Now, 92 years later,[President Trump has focused his energy on making things right.](https://substack.com/redirect/5fbb4a0a-b60c-434f-9eec-91c3d2042067?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **His next move has the power to trigger a financial reset that could shift trillions of dollars into the hands of the people.
**A provision buried in the U.S. Code Title 31, Section 5117 allows the U.S. Treasury to revalue America 's gold reserves from an outdated $42 per ounce to today's market price.**
That's a 72x increase!
**If activated, it could**
* **Reinforce America 's financial dominance**
* Reignite trust in value-backed money, making the dollar valuable again
* **Spark a modern day gold rush once the public understand their choices**

**Over 60 Million Americans are eligible to become a first wave benefactor in Trump 's Gold Reset.**
However, only those who download a copy of our **2025 Wealth Protection Guide** will know the simple steps needed to take part in this historic wealth reset.
**[Claim Your FREE Guide Now](https://substack.com/redirect/5fbb4a0a-b60c-434f-9eec-91c3d2042067?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and discover how to position yourself for this golden opportunity.**
[Claim your FREE guide HERE](https://substack.com/redirect/5fbb4a0a-b60c-434f-9eec-91c3d2042067?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
* * *
# NYC becomes the flashpoint of the Democrat Civil War as Hochul & Mamdani already clash
New York City Mayor-elect Zohran Mamdani, the 34-year-old socialist who stunned voters on Nov. 4, 2025, is **[igniting a fierce clash](https://substack.com/redirect/5682c4f3-2e05-4ff7-bf4c-7ee403cb4198?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** with Gov. Kathy Hochul by demanding state-backed tax hikes on millionaires and corporations to fund free buses, universal childcare, and city-run groceries--prompting raucous "Tax the rich!" chants and urgent pleas for Albany's approval amid NYC's soaring costs.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/5682c4f3-2e05-4ff7-bf4c-7ee403cb4198?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Mamdani 's Bold Tax Blueprint**: He proposes a 2% income levy on earners over $1 million and 11.5% corporate rate hike to match New Jersey, targeting revenue for affordability programs without middle-class burden.
* **Hochul Faces Progressive Fury** : Supporters disrupted her October 26 rally with anti-tax chants, forcing Mamdani's intervention; Hochul has vowed no hikes on everyday New Yorkers despite shared childcare goals.
* **National Allies Rally Support** : AOC and Bernie Sanders joined Mamdani at the Queens event, amplifying the pressure as Hochul dodges post-election queries on backing the plan.
* **GOP Slams Fiscal Folly** : Rep. Elise Stefanik blasted the socialist duo as "destroying New York," warning hikes could spur business exodus and deepen state-city budget tensions and emphasizing the widening split in the Democratic party between the Left and the Socialist Left.

* * *
* * *
# OpenAI CFO appears to demand a "pre-bailout" then backpedals FURIOUSLY
OpenAI's CFO Sarah Friar Wednesday **[appeared to call](https://substack.com/redirect/71188d55-c855-4d29-807e-564c406392bf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** for a federal guarantee to ease financing of massive AI chip investments for data centers, warning that without government backing, the U.S. risks falling behind in the global AI race.
* **CFO Friar Leads Charge** : Sarah Friar, OpenAI's finance chief, pitched the backstop idea at the WSJ Tech Live event in California to unlock billions in AI infrastructure funding.
* **Chip Investments Surge** : The plea targets enormous costs for AI-specific chips and data centers, essential for scaling models like ChatGPT amid exploding demand.
* **Global Race Looms** : Without federal support, Friar argued, U.S. firms could lose ground to international rivals accelerating AI dominance.
* **The Backlash Was Swift** : Analysts and commentators seized on the comments as evidence that, regardless of Sam Altman's Optimism, OpenAI has got too far out of their depth with their investments and is looking for government assistance.
* **CFO Posts A Retraction (of sorts):** Friar attempted to contain the damage with a post on social media claiming that her comments were misinterpreted
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/71188d55-c855-4d29-807e-564c406392bf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Too Little Too Late?:** However the damage was done and the retraction did little to quell the outrage online.

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/71188d55-c855-4d29-807e-564c406392bf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* * *
* * *
# Cowboys owner's billion dollar oil investment pays off huge
Explosive AI power demands from Big Tech data centers are igniting a natural gas frenzy in Texas, leaving Jerry Jones-backed Comstock Resources poised atop the **["holy grail" of untapped supply](https://substack.com/redirect/43a43a89-9df0-42a5-b735-ba64b7cc5efd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** in the challenging Haynesville Shale Basin.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/43a43a89-9df0-42a5-b735-ba64b7cc5efd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Strategic 2019 Acquisition Boost** : Comstock's $2.2 billion buy of Covey Park Energy exploded its Haynesville land holdings, unlocking vast reserves in the high-pressure Western region.
* **Wildcatting Boldness Conquers Depths** : Aggressive drilling at 14,000-19,000 feet depths positions Comstock as the lone leader, outpacing rivals amid extreme geological hurdles.
* **Prime Location Fuels Dual Demand** : Just 100 miles from Dallas and Houston, near LNG corridors, Comstock's assets perfectly supply both surging AI data centers and export facilities.
* **Jones ' Billion-Dollar Bet Pays Off**: The Cowboys owner's $1 billion investment, including $475 million for Covey Park, sets Comstock to dominate the AI-driven energy boom.

* * *
* * *
# **JP Morgan faces scrutiny over its "Debanking" of conservatives **
JPMorgan Chase revealed Wednesday it's **[under U.S. government scrutiny](https://substack.com/redirect/4db53f92-0e6d-4f99-9e6b-5f28b5735df5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** for allegedly debanking conservative clients, including President Donald Trump, who claimed the bank ousted him and his wife Melania over political ties after the 2021 Capitol riot.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/4db53f92-0e6d-4f99-9e6b-5f28b5735df5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Trump 's Cash Rejection Stuns**: In 2021, post-White House, Trump alleged JPMorgan rejected over $1 million in deposits, citing a 35-year relationship ended abruptly with a 20-day eviction notice.
* **Melania 's Post-Riot Ousting**: First Lady Melania Trump reported her account closure shortly after Jan. 6, 2021, riots, amplifying family-wide claims of political retaliation.
* **Broader Conservative Purge Exposed** : Under Biden, crypto firms and Second Amendment supporters faced similar debanking, blamed on "reputation risk" with policies favoring liberal alignments.
* **Regulatory Reckoning Looms** : Trump's August executive order targets fines for politicized practices in the banking industry

name: Troy Downing
subject: President Trump DOUBLES DOWN on Downing
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Troy Downing for Congress
body:
I’m counting on your endorsement to continue our America First mission. Please read.

[](https://trk.emails.troydowning.)Peter,
President Trump has officially endorsed my re-election campaign **AGAIN**.
He’s **DOUBLING** **DOWN** on his support because he knows how strong our shared commitment is to putting America First.[](https://trk.emails.troydowning.)Together, we’re standing strong to:🇺🇸 Support our Farmers and Ranchers
🇺🇸 Keep our Southern Border secure
🇺🇸 Make American Energy DOMINANT

name: Abdul El-Sayed
subject: Medicare for All isn't just a healthcare guarantee, it's a small business empowerment program
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Abdul for U
body:
[ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/30?refcode=&t=1001&akid=1239%2E251025%2ECCIeoI ]Abdul for US Senate [ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/30?refcode=&t=1002&akid=1239%2E251025%2ECCIeoI ]Abdul for US Senate

Peter,

When I talk about Medicare for All, I don’t just see it as a healthcare
program.

It’s a small business empowerment plan.

Because when we guarantee healthcare as a human right, we free small
business owners from one of their biggest burdens: paying for expensive
private insurance.

That’s money back in their pockets that they can use to hire workers,
raise wages, or invest in their communities.

The same goes for public schools. Every dollar we invest in our schools is
an investment in the next generation of local entrepreneurs and workers.

Meanwhile, giant corporations keep pitting state and local governments
against each other to see who will grant them the biggest tax breaks. And
politicians keep letting them win.

Peter, I’d rather
give tax breaks to small businesses than handouts to billion-dollar
corporations that don’t need them.

That’s what our economy could look like if we had leaders willing to say
so — and act on it.

I’m the only candidate in this race who supports Medicare for All, and the
only one who’s never taken a dime from corporate PACs. That’s no
coincidence.

But the health insurance industry and its allies are already bankrolling
my primary opponent to stop this movement from winning.

So if you believe healthcare should be a right, not a privilege, and if
you believe small businesses deserve a fair shot instead of billionaires
getting more breaks, I’m asking for your help:

[ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/507?c0=em-ms-aes-20251106-1-ak.5N0f46&amount=10&refcode=em20251106-1239&t=1004&akid=1239%2E251025%2ECCIeoI ]Chip in $10 or whatever you can to help us bring this
vision to the U.S. Senate.

[ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/507?c0=em-ms-aes-20251106-1-ak.5N0f46&amount=10&refcode=em20251106-1239&t=1023&akid=1239%2E251025%2ECCIeoI ]Donate $10 ››

This campaign isn’t funded by the corporations profiting off our broken
system. It’s funded by working people like you, because that’s who I’ll
fight for every single day.

Your friend in the fight,

Abdul

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. To contribute via check, please address it to Abdul for U.S. Senate, PO Box 126, St. Clair Shores, MI 48080.

name: Michael Baumgartner
subject: One year since our victory - and lots more to do!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Vote Baumgartner for Congress | www
body:
The fight continues...

| | | | | |
---
| | | | | | | | | | | | | | | |

It’s been one year since we won our election to Congress and I just wanted to drop a note to express my ongoing gratitude for your support!
With your help - we won the 2024 election with over sixty percent of the vote. The final tally showed we won all 12 counties and put up one of the best election results in the country!
---
|
---
| |

I promised during the campaign that I would work for commonsense conservative goals: to reduce taxes, secure the Southern border, and fight fentanyl and urban crime. I’ve kept those promises in my first term, along with work to ensure America is strong internationally, and that college sports are not overrun by big money.
This hard work has earned the respect of my colleagues in the House, including House Judiciary Chair Jim Jordan, who visited Spokane during the summer to show his support as well as President Trump, who sent over a nice endorsement this week.
---
|
---
| | There’s still much more work to do, to bring federal spending under control and return more power to local communities.
Honestly, I’m going to need your help in the months ahead. The Democrats are feeling buoyant after their wins on Tuesday in off-year elections in New York, New Jersey, Virginia and Pennsylvania.
In California, Governor Gavin Newsom’s gerrymandering plan to create five new Democrat seats is moving ahead after winning voter approval.
And, in Washington, D.C., the Democrats persist in keeping the government shut down, refusing to pass a short-term spending bill that would keep the lights on at federal agencies, and allow traffic controllers and TSA agents to be paid on time.
We should not kid ourselves about the opposition we will face. The federal bureaucracy has grown so large and the budget so out of balance that there is some federal footprint on virtually every walking trail, federal regulations on every building site, funding in all the nonprofit coffers, at every school and university. Pruning back these overreaching tendrils will not be easy, and many career civil servants, like my announced Democratic opponent, will be fighting hard to protect the Biden-era modus operandi.
It's true that midterm elections usually favor the party out of power. And, historically, the first time a Member of Congress faces re-election is their most difficult race, as money from across the country pours in to try to stop a Congressman setting down political roots.
But with your help, we will work hard in D.C. and on the campaign trail and match our 60% tally from a year ago.
It’s a long commute back and forth to D.C., but every time I board an early morning plane, I think about you and all the supporters in Eastern Washington who have put their trust in me, and I know the effort is worthwhile.
Thank you for all you do!
---
|
---
| | -Congressman Michael Baumgartner
---
| [DONATE](https://hotmail.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=91b93e6889137ccb3f3fef037&id=2a924f1727&e=5a45b9550e)
---
| | Paid for by Vote Baumgartner for Congress | www.votebaumgartner.com

Our mailing address is: Vote Baumgartner for Congress, PO Box 8508, Spokane, WA 99203
---
| | | | | | | | | You can [update your preferences](https://hotmail.us21.list-manage.com/profile?u=91b93e6889137ccb3f3fef037&id=62bfedeea6&e=5a45b9550e&c=019520684b) or [unsubscribe](https://hotmail.us21.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=91b93e6889137ccb3f3fef037&id=62bfedeea6&t=b&e=5a45b9550e&c=019520684b)
---

body:
President Trump’s administration just told a judge it will partially fund food assistance during the shutdown – but refuses to release the full $5 billion needed to keep SNAP benefits going.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118796079/580920147/32658279?refcode=BLU_EM_HSC_2025-11-06_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTY0MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwZmU0YzkyNC00MGJiLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRkNmYzMzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=GOkATcS3AHRmWRFHqS6jLKu82FrkdYWMr95f5InYtRA=)

| President Trump’s administration just told a judge it will partially fund food assistance during the shutdown – but refuses to release the full $5 billion needed to keep SNAP benefits going.

This is unconscionable.

The law is clear – these emergency funds exist precisely to make sure people can eat when Washington fails to do its job. But instead of helping hungry families, Trump’s USDA quietly deleted the rule that allows states to keep SNAP running during shutdowns.

[_**I’m calling on the Trump administration to fully fund SNAP now. Will you add your name to demand that the White House immediately release the $5 billion in emergency SNAP funds?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118796080/580920148/-1962642055?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTY0MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwZmU0YzkyNC00MGJiLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRkNmYzMzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=GOkATcS3AHRmWRFHqS6jLKu82FrkdYWMr95f5InYtRA=&emci=e8565153-ceb9-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&emdi=0fe4c924-40bb-f011-8e61-6045bdd6f338&ceid=750857)

name: Seth Magaziner
subject: Our opponent is trying to bring extremism to RI
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY Magaziner for Congress

# Thursday, November 6, 2025

**Good afternoon,** Cole here from _The Flyover_. With a new baby in the house--our third--I've been finding all sorts of ways to stretch a dollar. name: Voter Action Project
subject: Gotta fight this state-by-state
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
BCJ: State Bills Would Require Passport or Birth Certificate to Register to Vote [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=110625_1&amount=25]
Team,
We've told you that voter I.D. laws are unfairly biased against people of color, young people, and unmarried women. However, across the country, states are introducing bills to make these laws even MORE restrictive at an alarming rate.
The nonpartisan Brennan Center for Justice has the details: “ Lawmakers in at least 27 states have introduced...bills in 2025 that would require some or all voters to show citizenship documents to vote .” Not just when you register, but every single time you show up to cast a ballot.
While citizenship is required to vote, rigorous research shows time and again that instances of votes cast by non-citizens are extremely rare. At least 21 million citizens, however, do not have immediate access to the I.D. documents these laws would demand, including 3.1 million voters who lack documents entirely.
The suppressive SAVE Act is stalled in Congress. So opponents of voting rights have turned to the states. These bills aren’t motivated by a desire for free and fair elections – they’re just another voter suppression tactic.
We need your help to defend the right to vote in these 27 states and beyond. Will you chip in with $25 or whatever you can today to help us fight back against voter suppression and intimidation? >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=110625_1&amount=25]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=110625_1&amount=15] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=110625_1&amount=25] GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=110625_1&amount=50]
GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=110625_1&amount=100] GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=110625_1&amount=250] OTHER AMOUNT [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=110625_1&amount=35]
The problem is not only that these bills would block millions of Americans from voting, but also that these requirements to vote are a massive burden for election officials and many voters alike.
These intimidating requirements also come alongside other suppressive policies stocked into these bills: Mandatory voter purges, reduced early voting, closing polling places, restrictions on mail-in ballots, and so much more.
We need to speak with one loud, clear voice and tell every state legislature: We won't stand for any of these suppressive policies. Not now, not ever.
Opponents of voting rights aren't going to stop their work to restrict voting until we make them stop. Fuel our efforts to protect the right to vote with $25 or whatever you can today >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=110625_1&amount=25]
https://voteractionproject.org/ [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=110625_1&amount=20]
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=110625_1&amount=20]
Contributions or gifts to Integrity First Voter Action Project are not tax deductible as charitable contributions or as business expenses under IRC Section 162(3). Integrity First Voter Action Project is organized and operates under section 501(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code.
A copy of Integrity First Voter Action Project's latest financial report may be obtained by mail at Integrity First Voter Action Project, c/o Mele Brengarth & Assoc, PO Box 15845, Washington, DC 20003.
Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www.voteractionproject.org, and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
Integrity First Voter Action Project
P.O. Box 19294
Portland, OR 97280
United States
This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Emails are a crucial way to keep supporters up to date on the latest attacks on voting rights and our efforts to expand ballot access for all Americans. If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.everyaction.com/p/FQb7Rbo110qLPmSvMAn63Q2?unsubscribedata=3wXj5MNiAndARJRug%2FM0ue%2B%2FnIrRC7FiYGM61uodlZqSw8D3b%2BWLpJjxif1%2BGiprCDmsQJc%2B1YYWdHwV2iawmoAX24ghgghVxeNi6sVJ16eZyEiG3Nk9GEbKWMXKCuq1OZqzr5rjp%2BV1JfiA%2B%2Fq0OlysPIzTcGBUCm%2FewiptgyZ0RKrXTQhZ3Ao%2FyBtJodpJsMKLgp58lnGxI2W%2Bx%2Bh8gAXVqqotsxNkbHX8p5dVsMYPr9z15%2BOJtU%2B1fhBXKux91t4GRlWFVz2nd8WDTyiTy68Al%2BxciIOnU2n9xr%2BLFu7w7XhbJMBZWsnYPuEHkPFSdtI%2BUdbIfyxh1r5Hmti0nhVNUydktusWbc1d8IFpSWdGbMbJ1jb%2B8CSyNCQaotwknRZYg6d0v4nwL8LDdTXN%2FxzVh1B9DNp7d3U6IXaJgeo%3D .

Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? name: Team Katie Porter
subject: We can’t let a Republican become California’s next governor.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Governor 2026
body:
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21486?t=1&akid=40370%2E3422442%2EC-ndQP)
No BS: we have some good news and bad news:
The good news: according to a recent poll, Katie Porter is the leading Democratic candidate for California governor:
Steve Hilton: 16%
Katie Porter: 15%
Undecided: 39%
_Source: Emerson College Polling_
**But here’s the bad news: Republican Steve Hilton is 1% ahead of Katie** with 39% of voters undecided. We are going to fight for every single undecided vote because so much is on the line for the future of our state.
Katie is running to fight for working families—to lower costs on housing, childcare, and groceries. California deserves someone who will stand up to Trump and protect our basic rights—that’s Katie.
**We can’t let a Republican become California’s next governor.** So today we’re asking:
**[Can you chip in $5 to our campaign today to help us reach undecided voters and help Katie take back the lead in this race?](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20251106_1_ak.NkStlc&refcode=em20251106-40370&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=40370%2E3422442%2EC-ndQP)**

Thank you,
Team Katie Porter

Katie is running to be California’s next governor because California and our country need a leader who will stand up to Trump to lower costs, protect our rights, and combat climate change. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4g-em-footer?refcode=em20251106-40370&amounts=&t=23&refcode2=40370_3422442_C-ndQP&akid=40370%2E3422442%2EC-ndQP)**

name: JB Pritzker
subject: A proven fighter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chris Pappas for Senate
body:
[](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/221?c0=em_ms_cp_20251106_1_pritzker.skn2Ac&refcode=em20251106-1&t=1&akid=3326%2E582294%2E-qCqex)
JB Pritzker here.
Let me cut to the chase: **New Hampshire’s Senate race could decide whether Democrats take back the majority — _and Republicans know it._**
Donald Trump and the MAGA GOP see this seat as one of their best shots to expand their power.
They’ve already got a Republican governor in New Hampshire, and they’re betting they can flip the Senate seat next. If they win here, our chances of taking back the Senate drop to zero.
_We can’t let that happen._
Chris Pappas is a proven fighter for working families — taking on Big Pharma, defending reproductive freedom, and making sure our government actually works for the people it serves.
I’ve seen what happens when leaders like Chris have the platform and the power to make change — and _I want him in the Senate._
I know Chris can win, but there’s no way around it: **_This isn’t a safe blue seat, folks._**
The GOP is ready to flood New Hampshire with attack ads and dark money. The only way to beat them is with early, grassroots support. That’s where you come in:
**[If you can, please make a $5 donation right now to help Chris Pappas win in New Hampshire and put Democrats on the path to a Senate majority.](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/221?c0=em_ms_cp_20251106_1_pritzker.skn2Ac&refcode=em20251106-1&t=2&akid=3326%2E582294%2E-qCqex)**

Thank you for standing up,
JB Pritzker
Governor of Illinois
**New Hampshire is poised to be one of the closest Senate races in the country in 2026. Will you chip in today to help Chris stop the Republicans from flipping the Granite State red and expanding their Senate majority?**

Randy grew up in a working-class immigrant family in California’s Central Valley. His parents ran an auto shop, and like so many families in the Valley, they relied on public schools and Medicaid to get by.
He went from working in that small shop to earning a PhD. Now, he’s back home, running for Congress to fight for families like his. All without taking a single dime of corporate PAC money.
**Prop 50 changes the game.**
With Prop 50’s passage, tens of thousands of new voters are being added to CA-22 -- voters who’ve never heard Randy’s name.
Republicans are already flooding the area with dark money to buy this seat. But we have something stronger: people power.
If Democrats organize NOW, we can flip this seat -- and flip the House.

clean_body: | = Hi, David Hogg here, Parkland survivor and co-founder of Leaders We Deserve. Prop 50 passed -- instantly making CA-22 one of the most competitive congressional districts in the country. = | | = Randy grew up in a working-class immigrant family in California’s Central Valley. His parents ran an auto shop, and like so many families in the Valley, they relied on public schools and Medicaid to get by. He went from working in that small shop to earning a PhD. Now, he’s back home, running for Congress to fight for families like his. All without taking a single dime of corporate PAC money. Prop 50 changes the game. With Prop 50’s passage, tens of thousands of new voters are being added to CA-22 -- voters who’ve never heard Randy’s name. Republicans are already flooding the area with dark money to buy this seat. But we have something stronger: people power. name: Jackson Lahmeyer
subject: POLITICALLY INCORRECT - The TRUTH About Islam
body:
This is not meant for POLITICAL CORRECTNESS but for TRUTH: When I was a student at Oral Roberts University, I studied theology for my Undergraduate a
| | [](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=26161887.13530.1.b9d3ca2ee116de5ddbe5f9aeff2998e9) |
---
This is not meant for POLITICAL CORRECTNESS but for TRUTH: When I was a student at **Oral Roberts University** , I studied theology for my Undergraduate and Graduate Degrees. One class that I took (so glad I did) was over **Islam** and the **Quran**. This course was over the history of **Islam** as well as the teachings contained within the **Quran**. I was required to read the **Quran** as well as much of the Hadith. Most people have never opened up a **Quran** , let alone read through it. While studying the history of **Islam** and actually reading the **Quran** , I was blown away... **First** , **Islam** _has never been or ever will be_ a religion of peace. It advances by the sword. President Bush was WRONG! **Secondly** , the values of **Islam** and the values of **Western Civilization** are _irreconcilable_. Oil and water. Wife beating, sex slavery, child abuse and much darker characteristics of **Islam** and the teachings of the Quran and the Hadith can never work within the framework of **Western Civilization**. **Thirdly** , the founder of **Islam** and the founder of **Christianity** could not be more different. **Muhammad** according to Islamic sources was a **brutal war lord** and an individual who was betrothed to a 6 year old girl named Aisha and consummated the marriage when she was 9 years old. _Let me repeat_ : **Muhammad** consummated the marriage when she was 9 years old! These are not my claims, but Islamic claims. The push back to what I just wrote is that in our modern world there are moderate Muslims. That is TRUE. Not every Muslim is a radical Jihadist. In fact, there are some very upstanding and moral Muslims that I personally know. However, every radical Jihadist is a Muslim and there are millions of them who are sold-out on a Islamic Eschatological goal of a global caliphate. **Islam** does not want to simply conquer the Middle East. The Western World is the prize and America is the crown jewel. The two GREATEST persecutors of Christians today happen to be **Islam** and **Communism**. Islamic countries like Iran and Nigeria as well as Communist countries like North Korea and China are the leaders in the deaths of thousands of Christians every year. The election of a **Communist Muslim** as the Mayor of New York City is beyond concerning... Last year, I delivered a message entitled "[The Battle Between The Cross & The Crescent For America](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=26161887.13531.1.00eb2ee3c0a1c54a5946283212363684)" which you can watch here: It is so important that we pray for our brothers and sisters in the Lord who are persecuted in Islamic and Communist countries right now but that we also pray for our nation because there is a battle for the soul of America between [the Cross and the Crescent](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=26161887.13532.1.387086344de0be8f65ec9901f6926f0b). Thank you, Pastor Jackson Lahmeyer

Recently, I woke up today with a really cool notification... My **FIRST** book, ["_Chasing After The Wind_ "](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=26161887.13540.1.12538de7acf4e859694f2636193279fa) is out and has reached **#12 in Religion/ Christian Living / Personal Growth**! **I have been blown away by the response!** THANK YOU SO MUCH! If you have not ordered your copy you can still get the **Limited Edition Signed Book** here: _[*"Chasing After The Wind"*](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=26161887.13541.1.e0b262f8f9c8504173e86def33d930ec)_ It is also available on [Amazon](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=26161887.13542.1.5508e5648c997a7931224596fdb4c46e)\+ [Barnes & Noble](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=26161887.13543.1.dd704b11e1f518d65f8ff8557eca9c8f). **Amazon** : [https://www.amazon.com/Chasing-After-Wind-Catching-Everybody/dp/B0F7M4GK26](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=26161887.13544.1.38fef2b547d9b328b5daf1bdf3d7fecd) **Barnes & Noble**: [https://www.barnesandnoble.com/w/chasing-after-the-wind-jackson-lahmeyer/1147391019](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=26161887.13545.1.2784b162d206f6114b72f7690101914a) I really want to thank **MLB LEGEND Darryl Strawberry** for writing the Foreword and becoming vulnerable by sharing his story. The Foreword is worth getting the book, I promise. This book is 200 pages and I am confident will change your life as it has mine. In ["Chasing After The Wind"](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=26161887.13546.1.a63617a7a2e1ba48543487f9e363dc04) I share VERY personal stories and I decided to get real and raw with the heartbreaks and triumphs I have experienced.

If not, you may miss this **once-in-a-lifetime opportunity.**
**Because of the government shutdown, I can only keep this position open until midnight, so act fast because I really want YOU to be my White House Advisor!**

### I love you & I’m crossing my fingers that you still feel the same way about me.

body:
While Washington remains gridlocked, Dr. Raul Ruiz is committed to serving and helping constituents with the problems they have with the federal government and cutting red tape. If you’d like to unsubscribe, [click here.](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/dAfRehWDrkq5GdVTndjWgA2?unsubscribedata=%2BdYEccJMEY82UxPV7rQ1OyKXgVvh%2FGki7U9EcyP0GDdPJtD5ZsVgUNo9ocG7OHgJdjnVgo19SHBNVhtIgl9QmcRPm3TuSApobsbXgvUDSzCjb%2BDHnEVRjIgd8SrAhkVmnTTaddzwCcbCemxx8i%2BSDSD58fKRuJVnvoLlb3cI4nR7JbrlE9oLfFKhOliY6ajEbgtQwBzMJo80d7MC8C0M%2BpPRZxOA8hcc8EP7mEc%2BlFHW3ccDJYtk0nVD4ziJcP9obaevBiGVVzDfYttBIYdjkneFVHJzGtlNOOmRRsZ0f%2FphIEvfhB%2FMQyH3iOj0MHOg4BIgQ3DlJh8Sr2bY0csFD6MbVNE6b%2BSy22aov3vNxztiVNVFyL7Beu5bX45MTcZ2tJnSGiDGOvTly09FKh5OKxG8LOGehoCx9V2qNyfiseSmwE6lStwflEzUmQlprE%2BH)

Did you see this email from Dr. Ruiz? The government shutdown is causing families to go hungry. **Let’s be clear about one thing:** The government can afford to fund SNAP. It can afford to fund healthcare. Instead, this Republican-led administration and Congress have decided that a $230 million payout for Trump and tax cuts for his billionaire buddies should take priority over people’s lives. Dr. Ruiz’s office remains open to help Californians navigate the challenges of the shutdown — but Republicans need to come to the table to negotiate. He’s not letting up the pressure, but together we can send a stronger message. [We need 5,000 signatures by midnight. Will you stand with Dr. Ruiz in demanding Republicans end the shutdown?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118798250/580927395/-553215934?source=SBD_RR_EM_FR_110625_SNAPFWD_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDcvMS8xMDc4NzAiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZjA0ZDgxMmEtNDZiYi1mMDExLThlNjEtNjA0NWJkZDZmMzM4IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=NlEJwOgw1lvinaLFYYyiO4SOFhkA-Y9L2QNLZjtyIQc=&emci=0759f822-46bb-f011-8e61-6045bdd6f338&emdi=f04d812a-46bb-f011-8e61-6045bdd6f338&ceid=4131986) | | **[ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118798251/580927396/-553215934?source=SBD_RR_EM_FR_110625_SNAPFWD_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDcvMS8xMDc4NzAiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZjA0ZDgxMmEtNDZiYi1mMDExLThlNjEtNjA0NWJkZDZmMzM4IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=NlEJwOgw1lvinaLFYYyiO4SOFhkA-Y9L2QNLZjtyIQc=&emci=0759f822-46bb-f011-8e61-6045bdd6f338&emdi=f04d812a-46bb-f011-8e61-6045bdd6f338&ceid=4131986)**

Thank you,
Ruiz HQ
**\---------- Forwarding message from Dr. Raul Ruiz ----------**
It’s Raul.
Thanks to you, we reached our end-of-month fundraising goal — and I couldn’t be more grateful.
You’ve always shown up when it matters most, standing with me to protect and strengthen CA-25. Together, we’ve accomplished so much for our community. But right now, many of our friends and neighbors are facing incredibly tough times.
We’re now in the second month of the Republican government shutdown. And as of November 1, families struggling to feed their kids will no longer receive the help they need from the SNAP food assistance program.
That means millions of people across the country — and thousands right here at home — are suddenly without the help they rely on to feed their families.
Parents are wondering how they’ll put dinner on the table. Kids are showing up to school hungry. And all of it is completely avoidable.
Donald Trump and Congressional Republicans could fund SNAP today. They could come to the table, reopen the government, and make sure no child goes hungry — but so far, they’ve refused.
[So I’m asking you: will you add your name to demand that Donald Trump and his allies in Congress fund SNAP and end this shutdown?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118798252/580927397/-553215934?source=SBD_RR_EM_FR_110625_SNAPFWD_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDcvMS8xMDc4NzAiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZjA0ZDgxMmEtNDZiYi1mMDExLThlNjEtNjA0NWJkZDZmMzM4IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=NlEJwOgw1lvinaLFYYyiO4SOFhkA-Y9L2QNLZjtyIQc=&emci=0759f822-46bb-f011-8e61-6045bdd6f338&emdi=f04d812a-46bb-f011-8e61-6045bdd6f338&ceid=4131986)
| **[ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118798253/580927398/-553215934?source=SBD_RR_EM_FR_110625_SNAPFWD_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDcvMS8xMDc4NzAiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZjA0ZDgxMmEtNDZiYi1mMDExLThlNjEtNjA0NWJkZDZmMzM4IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=NlEJwOgw1lvinaLFYYyiO4SOFhkA-Y9L2QNLZjtyIQc=&emci=0759f822-46bb-f011-8e61-6045bdd6f338&emdi=f04d812a-46bb-f011-8e61-6045bdd6f338&ceid=4131986)**
---
First, I want to assure you that my offices will continue to remain open throughout the shutdown. My team and I are here to help you and your family — whether you need assistance with federal benefits, services, or simply need to be heard.
We’re in this fight together, and I’ll never stop working to make government work for you.
Thank you,

clean_body: While Washington remains gridlocked, Dr. Raul Ruiz is committed to serving and helping constituents with the problems they have with the federal government and cutting red tape. If you’d like to unsubscribe, | | Did you see this email from Dr. Ruiz? The government shutdown is causing families to go hungry. Let’s be clear about one thing: The government can afford to fund SNAP. It can afford to fund healthcare. Instead, this Republican-led administration and Congress have decided that a $230 million payout for Trump and tax cuts for his billionaire buddies should take priority over people’s lives. Dr. Ruiz’s office remains open to help Californians navigate the challenges of the shutdown — but Republicans need to come to the table to negotiate. He’s not letting up the pressure, but together we can send a stronger message. | | Thank you, Ruiz HQ \---------- Forwarding message from Dr. Raul Ruiz ---------- It’s Raul. Thanks to you, we reached our end-of-month fundraising goal — and I couldn’t be more grateful. You’ve always shown up when it matters most, standing with me to protect and strengthen CA-25. Together, we’ve accomplished so much for our community. But right now, many of our friends and neighbors are facing incredibly tough times. We’re now in the second month of the Republican government shutdown. And as of November 1, families struggling to feed their kids will no longer receive the help they need from the SNAP food assistance program. That means millions of people across the country — and thousands right here at home — are suddenly without the help they rely on to feed their families. Parents are wondering how they’ll put dinner on the table. Kids are showing up to school hungry. And all of it is completely avoidable. Donald Trump and Congressional Republicans could fund SNAP today. They could come to the table, reopen the government, and make sure no child goes hungry — but so far, they’ve refused. | First, I want to assure you that my offices will continue to remain open throughout the shutdown. My team and I are here to help you and your family — whether you need assistance with federal benefits, services, or simply need to be heard. We’re in this fight together, and I’ll never stop working to make government work for you. body:
We're pretty sure you didn't wake up this morning, jump out of bed and say "Today is the day I am going to split a donation between Jon Ossoff's U.S. Senate campaign and Linda Sánchez"

| Peter—

We're pretty sure you didn't wake up this morning, jump out of bed and say "Today is the day I am going to split a donation between Jon Ossoff's U.S. Senate campaign and Linda Sánchez"

_(If you did, great! Here’s the link and thank you in advance:[_**https://secure.actblue.com/donate/blu-san-ossoff-2025-em?refcode=BLU_EM_SAN_2025-11-06_1 &amount=25**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118798305/580927617/1184209682?refcode=BLU_EM_SAN_2025-11-06_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85ODI2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxZmFlZTM2ZC00NmJiLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRkNmYzMzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DYGmaI7ovnVUi5eEQaPyDbiYaoDo_1ltiO2xxDpYcdU=))_

Otherwise, please give us a second to explain why we’re asking you to chip in and why particularly right now.

Jon won his last election by building the largest on-the-ground movement that Georgia had ever seen.

Doing it again — and going _even bigger_ — is not going to be easy. It is going to take sustained effort from now through Election Day. And we have to get to work right away.

Unfortunately, a lot of people think maybe it's too early to give. Or they'll wait until later. We get that.

But the truth is _early money to a campaign is more important than a donation at any other time._

Your donation now will help us build the strong foundation we need to win, and ensure we’re ready for anything that comes our way on this campaign, from Donald Trump, our MAGA opponents, or anyone else. **So what do you say?

[_Can Jon count on you to chip in $25, or any amount to his campaign and Linda Sánchez today? We’ve got a tough road ahead of us, and every dollar makes a difference._](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118798310/580927623/1184209682?refcode=BLU_EM_SAN_2025-11-06_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85ODI2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxZmFlZTM2ZC00NmJiLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRkNmYzMzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DYGmaI7ovnVUi5eEQaPyDbiYaoDo_1ltiO2xxDpYcdU=)**

body:
It’s not energy vs. the planet — it’s both.

[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118798831/580929120/1239926708?refcode=em_WEI_FR_GreenEnergy_110625&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2NjhjNGM2My00OGJiLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRkNmYzMzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=O7drL5yy_svjiMz7quoccvp5ME6awiMOo91Gi7LKWVg=&emci=6bc74c0e-2fbb-f011-8194-6045bda9d96b&emdi=668c4c63-48bb-f011-8e61-6045bdd6f338&ceid=51844520) Friend, when I think about my kids’ future, I think about our energy future here in Colorado. We’ve got a few important priorities to cover: Clean energy sources we can rely on for generations to come. Creating and keeping good-paying jobs. _And_ keeping our utility bills manageable. **As governor, my plan will deliver for Coloradans.** **[Colorado is already a green energy technology hub. I’m ready and willing to fight for all Coloradans to reap the benefits of a just transition to renewable energy. If that’s a plan you can get behind, please consider supporting our campaign with $5, $25, or whatever works for you.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118798834/580929127/109727453?amount=25&refcode=em_WEI_FR_GreenEnergy_110625&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2NjhjNGM2My00OGJiLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRkNmYzMzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=O7drL5yy_svjiMz7quoccvp5ME6awiMOo91Gi7LKWVg=)** As the Trump administration denies climate change and undermines our shift to a clean energy future, our state needs to keep leading this transition. These are just a few examples of how we’ll do that:
* **🔋 Leading in solar and wind** : As governor, I’ll engage with local governments, accelerate siting projects, and support these options in a timely way — especially because right now, they’re the lowest-cost sources of energy for our state.
* **🔋 Adopting safe energy storage** : By promoting innovative financing tools and encouraging utilities to broaden battery use cases, we can defer some more expensive upgrades during this urgent transition. We’ll work hard to create a more adaptable, resilient grid with lower energy costs for consumers.
* **🔋 Shifting to electric vehicles** : As governor, we will continue to support this important transition and support consumers in making the switch.
* **🔋 Blazing new trails with geothermal** : My administration will establish a Geothermal Development Authority to coordinate and support activities across the state. We’ll help everyone make the most of this new frontier, especially in rural Colorado, where there is great promise for geothermal energy development in communities where coal plants are closed.

I could keep going, but my point is this: Colorado will continue to lead the way for the entire country on clean energy. **[We need you in this fight for our future. Can you support our grassroots movement with a contribution right now?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118798836/580929131/109727453?refcode=em_WEI_FR_GreenEnergy_110625&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2NjhjNGM2My00OGJiLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRkNmYzMzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=O7drL5yy_svjiMz7quoccvp5ME6awiMOo91Gi7LKWVg=)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | | [Donate $10 →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118798838/580929132/109727453?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=em_WEI_FR_GreenEnergy_110625&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2NjhjNGM2My00OGJiLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRkNmYzMzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=O7drL5yy_svjiMz7quoccvp5ME6awiMOo91Gi7LKWVg=)

clean_body: It’s not energy vs. the planet — it’s both. Friend, when I think about my kids’ future, I think about our energy future here in Colorado. We’ve got a few important priorities to cover: Clean energy sources we can rely on for generations to come. Creating and keeping good-paying jobs. And keeping our utility bills manageable. As governor, my plan will deliver for Coloradans. = As the Trump administration denies climate change and undermines our shift to a clean energy future, our state needs to keep leading this transition. These are just a few examples of how we’ll do that: 🔋 Leading in solar and wind : As governor, I’ll engage with local governments, accelerate siting projects, and support these options in a timely way — especially because right now, they’re the lowest-cost sources of energy for our state. 🔋 Adopting safe energy storage : By promoting innovative financing tools and encouraging utilities to broaden battery use cases, we can defer some more expensive upgrades during this urgent transition. We’ll work hard to create a more adaptable, resilient grid with lower energy costs for consumers. 🔋 Shifting to electric vehicles : As governor, we will continue to support this important transition and support consumers in making the switch. 🔋 Blazing new trails with geothermal : My administration will establish a Geothermal Development Authority to coordinate and support activities across the state. We’ll help everyone make the most of this new frontier, especially in rural Colorado, where there is great promise for geothermal energy development in communities where coal plants are closed. I could keep going, but my point is this: Colorado will continue to lead the way for the entire country on clean energy. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | = | = | | | = | = | | | = | = Let’s show everyone how it’s done, Phil | Make sure this email goes to your inbox. Hi friend, we just closed the books on the first month of our campaign, and I am so proud and grateful to have you with us. Since launching our campaign on October 15th, tens of thousands of people across Massachusetts and the nation have joined our fight for new, bold leadership. You've shared our message on social media, signed up to volunteer with our team, and chipped in to help us build our campaign. It means the world to have your support, and we need it to win this race. Our primary election is ten months away, and there's a lot of work to be done. name: Steve Kerrigan
subject: Celebrate the Season with Us at the Annual MassDems Holiday Reception!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Massachusetts Democratic State Committee
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP31/1/93809/images/Logo.png]
Dear Peter,
As we wrap up an incredible year for Massachusetts Democrats, I’m excited to invite you to join Chair Steve Kerrigan, Governor Maura Healey, and Lieutenant Governor Kim Driscoll for our annual MassDems Holiday Reception!
Let’s come together to celebrate our shared accomplishments, reconnect with friends and colleagues, and look ahead to the important work that awaits us in 2026.
This year’s event will take place on Monday, December 15th, at 5:30 PM at the Omni Parker House – Alcott Junior Ballroom, 60 School Street, Boston, MA 02108.
Your support fuels the work that powers our victories, from local campaigns to statewide efforts, and ensures we’re ready to meet the challenges and opportunities of 2026.
You’ll find full event details on the invitation below, and you can RSVP by clicking the button here:
RSVP For MDP Holiday Party [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mdp-holiday2025]

Steve Kerrigan,

I’ve been in politics a long damn time. I’ve heard a lot of nonsense. But nothing like the nonsense coming out of Washington these days.
Republicans control BOTH houses of Congress AND the White House and are STILL blaming Democrats for their government shutdown. Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem even tried to force airports to play videos blaming Democrats for both the shutdown and flight delays. _You just can’t make this s*** up._
Look, I know you’re busy. But this is serious. If we want a government that actually gives a damn about people, we’ve got to flip the Senate. That means beating folks like Susan Collins, Jon Husted, and every other senator who thinks your health care is expendable.
So here’s what I’m asking:
**[Can you chip in $5 or more right now to Flip the Senate and help build the grassroots movement we need to end this cruelty and take back power?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118798997/580929753/-736798564?amount=5&refcode=AU_SMP_FR_GEN_GEN_AC30_20251106_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82ODY3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0YTA0NWRhYS00OGJiLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRkNmYzMzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=d4WVlbFUeCzaQZDvlq-NbpjsVZwitOo_WpOim3E_oI0=)**
Every dollar helps us fight back. Let’s make damn sure Republicans’ actions come back to haunt them on Election Day.
— James Carville

name: Martin Heinrich
subject: I’m proud to endorse Mallory McMorrow
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Martin Heinrich for Senate
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118799093/580929999/-272948131?refcode=AU_MTH_FR_TAN_GEN_AC_20251106_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNzBlMmE4ZS00OWJiLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRkNmYzMzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=iGbDWT7n7Yqtw5veZjpy39_YkjiIAnahs_d6-GeE-M4=&emci=21ef4b88-45bb-f011-8e61-6045bdd6f338&emdi=e70e2a8e-49bb-f011-8e61-6045bdd6f338&ceid=252045)
---
Hey there, I don’t usually wade into other states’ primaries. But today I’m making an exception in endorsing Mallory McMorrow, and I need to tell you why. Some folks in D.C. have decided they’re going to try to sway this race. The kind of establishment meddling that drives people crazy: Washington insiders thinking they know better than voters who should represent them. I can’t stand that kind of politics. And I refuse to sit on the sidelines and watch it happen. **That’s why I’m endorsing Mallory McMorrow. ** **If I’ve already told you enough, and you’re ready to support Mallory too, you can split a donation with my personal link here:[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mth-em-mcmorrow](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118799094/580930000/1164759976?refcode=AU_MTH_FR_TAN_GEN_AC_20251106_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNzBlMmE4ZS00OWJiLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRkNmYzMzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=iGbDWT7n7Yqtw5veZjpy39_YkjiIAnahs_d6-GeE-M4=)** Mallory has fought to expand the earned income tax credit in Michigan, held the line on clean water access, and taken on price gouging that’s squeezing working families. She doesn't just talk about fighting for people, she actually does it – even when it's hard, and especially when it matters most. Michiganders couldn’t ask for a stronger champion for their rights and freedoms, and I couldn’t imagine a better partner to join me in the U.S. Senate. But if you need some more info, here’s what’s at stake: Republicans see Michigan as their golden ticket to flipping a Democratic seat **red**. They’ve seen that Mallory was outspent last quarter by Mike Rogers, and they’ve seen the polling that shows she’s the only candidate in this primary who can beat him in a head-to-head general election match up. So yeah, they’re scared. And on top of that, she’s built a movement of people who are fed up with the BS they’ve seen in Washington. A movement of people who are ready to fight for a government that actually works for them. People who are sick and tired of the Washington establishment misreading the moment and running scared instead of being bold. [_**Please help me make sure Mallory has what she needs to win by splitting a donation between our campaigns today. Every bit helps — seriously.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118799095/580930001/1164759976?refcode=AU_MTH_FR_TAN_GEN_AC_20251106_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNzBlMmE4ZS00OWJiLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRkNmYzMzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=iGbDWT7n7Yqtw5veZjpy39_YkjiIAnahs_d6-GeE-M4=) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation to Mallory McMorrow and Martin Heinrich will go through immediately:_ | | [CHIP IN $5 NOW](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118799096/580930002/1164759976?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=AU_MTH_FR_TAN_GEN_AC_20251106_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNzBlMmE4ZS00OWJiLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRkNmYzMzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=iGbDWT7n7Yqtw5veZjpy39_YkjiIAnahs_d6-GeE-M4=)

---
When I first ran for office, I didn’t have a long background in politics or a family history of campaigning.
I saw my community needed someone to step up, and I couldn’t sit back.
That’s the kind of leader Mallory is. She’s not backed by corporate donors; she’s counting on grassroots supporters who believe in her vision for the New American Dream.
I know that Mallory will be an incredible champion for her state in the U.S. Senate, just as she’s been in the State Senate. Now more than ever, we need strong voices who aren’t afraid to speak out and call out the BS when they see it. _That’s Mallory. _
[_**So, join me in supporting Mallory for Senate. Can you split a donation between our campaigns today?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118799102/580930008/1164759976?refcode=AU_MTH_FR_TAN_GEN_AC_20251106_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNzBlMmE4ZS00OWJiLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRkNmYzMzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=iGbDWT7n7Yqtw5veZjpy39_YkjiIAnahs_d6-GeE-M4=)
Thanks for being part of this. We’re in it together.
Martin

name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: The Mountain Minute: 11/6/2025
body:
Inside the 2025 Colorado elections: What voters rewarded, rejected—and why it matters
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-mQ/s6l1f926028)
---
**PRESENTED BY[ROCKYMOUNTAINVOICE.COM](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-mQ/sfs46409906)**
| | Hey folks,
We skipped our usual Mountain Minute yesterday because we spent the day **breaking down key races, turnout trend, and voter shift across Colorado** — so you wouldn’t have to. Here’s the quick version... Coloradans showed they’re still *kinda* paying attention. Voters rewarded engagement and punished drift, with turnout around 43% statewide. Conservatives notched a few wins, but the left’s union-backed organizing machine was more coordinated — and better funded — than ever. **Read our full breakdown below or[click here](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-mQ/s1m37628e73). **
Listen to today's Mountain Minute podcast [here](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-mQ/s6n8f3a9aad), or watch on [YouTube](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-mQ/s9mec05bc66) or [Rumble](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-mQ/sck3573f5f4). Here is a summary of today's top stories:
🏔️ **Here in Colorado...** Denver [approved Mayor Mike Johnston’s $935 million “Vibrant Denver” bond](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-mQ/s0nd76f5ff1) while critics warned it deepens debt and centralizes power. Meanwhile, 40+ counties led by Mesa County’s Bobbie Daniel are fighting [“unfunded mandates” from Governor Polis](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-mQ/sas99b06ef8) that cost local taxpayers millions. In Montrose County, [conservatives swept school board races](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-mQ/s0j349033f4), recalled Commissioner Scott Mijares, and rejected new spending measures. And statewide, AG Phil Weiser’s [push for mid-cycle redistricting](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-mQ/s5pa7e3bd3e) to “Trump-proof” Colorado drew sharp criticism as a partisan power grab.
🇺🇸 **On the National Stage...** America’s divide deepened as [Republicans faced major losses](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-mQ/s2hce598885) in Virginia and New Jersey, where Democrats flipped school boards, legislatures, and local offices—driven by high blue-state turnout and backlash over federal dysfunction. In New York City, leaked documents showed the Democratic Socialists of America preparing to pressure incoming socialist mayor Zohran Mamdani to push an anti-Israel, anti-capitalist agenda—underscoring how [far-left movements are reshaping local politics](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-mQ/s8v26f7f6bc) heading into 2026.
🗣️ **Commentary Spotlight...** Bobbie Daniel’s [“It’s Time for Fiscal Common Sense in Colorado”](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-mQ/s6p7b3864d8) rebukes the state’s unfunded mandates, likening them to bad business and urging lawmakers to “fund it or fix it.” Pastor Drake Hunter’s [“Grama’s Magnifying Glass”](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-mQ/s4of8fd7eec) offers a spiritual reminder that what Coloradans focus on—truth or division—will define the state’s future. Together, their message is clear: Colorado’s renewal depends on [fiscal discipline and moral clarity](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-mQ/s3q6847a182).
This is the Mountain Minute for Thursday, November 6, 2025.
– Team RMV
---
|
---
**TODAY'S TOP STORIES**
**[Inside the 2025 Colorado elections: What voters rewarded, rejected—and why it matters](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-mQ/s4sb5e33eae)** BY RMV EDITORIAL BOARD, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-mQ/segeb605b35)
[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-mQ/sfp9a3198fc)
Colorado voters made their message plain this year, though not all spoke with one voice. In city halls and school races they favored those who stayed engaged, turned away those who coasted and reminded every leader that trust has an expiration date.
The RMV editorial board concludes that 2025 revealed both the strength of the left’s organization and the right’s fragmentation—union-backed PACs outspent grassroots efforts ten to one, flipping long-held conservative regions like Douglas and Mesa counties. The lesson heading into 2026, RMV warns, is one of unity and urgency: conservatives must rebuild infrastructure, align message and money, and fight on the terrain of meaning—not just policy—if they hope to compete in a Colorado increasingly shaped by emotional, organized, and progressive activism.
**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-mQ/sbq06033465)**
|
---
**[A Conservative Tide in Montrose County Education, Tempered by a Disturbing Underbelly](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-mQ/s7o89686cca)** BY MICHAEL J BADAGLIACCO "MJB", [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-mQ/s6p6695050b)
* **Conservatives sweep Montrose County school board races:** Voters backed candidates Neisha Balleck, Scott Scarborough, Tiffany Vincent, and Shane Daly—each campaigning on restoring academics, parental rights, and fiscal discipline—marking a decisive rejection of progressive education policies and “woke” agendas.
* **Commissioner Scott Mijares recalled amid “dark money” influence:** Mijares was ousted 52.5% to 47.5%, replaced by unaffiliated candidate Kirstin Copeland. The recall, driven more by personality conflicts than policy, highlighted growing tension between leadership style and community expectations in conservative Montrose.
* **Fiscal restraint defines local ballot results:** Voters rejected the Montrose City Charter Amendment and Library District Measures 6A and 6B, signaling resistance to higher taxes or expanded government spending and reaffirming a community-wide demand for accountability and limited government.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-mQ/s5r4b5518ba)**
|
---
The Rocky Mountain Voice is a 501(c)(3) that exists to promote informed civic engagement and the principles of self-governance through enterprise reporting on public policy, politics, government, and culture throughout the state of Colorado. The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support!
[DONATE TO ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-mQ/s7g70c29013)
|
---
**[Colorado Democrats Eye New Map Powers as Weiser Calls for Constitutional Change](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-mQ/sel6c21d86d)** [COMPLETE COLORADOO](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-mQ/s1p139066b6)
* **Weiser backs mid-cycle redistricting:** Colorado Attorney General Phil Weiser became the first statewide official—and a declared gubernatorial candidate—to support a constitutional amendment allowing Colorado to redraw congressional maps mid-decade to “Trump-proof” the state if GOP-led states gerrymander their maps.
* **Proposed shift from Amendment Y’s safeguards:** The 2018 voter-approved Amendment Y established a bipartisan 12-member commission to prevent partisan gerrymandering, but Weiser’s proposal would override that system, enabling Democrats to redraw districts before 2028.
* **Critics call it a partisan power grab:** Former House Speaker Frank McNulty denounced the plan as “a raw power play,” arguing Democrats are trying to secure more congressional seats for political gain amid fears of losing ground nationally.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-mQ/s0kf011ed60)**
|
---
**ONLY ON ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE ⤵️ **
**[Grama’s Magnifying Glass: Refocusing the Voice of Colorado](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-mQ/sbl87e0a21e)**
**BY DRAKE HUNTER | COMMENTARY | NOVEMBER 5** _While sitting in the waiting area at MD Anderson Cancer Center in Houston, I heard the characteristic sounds of hospital life—beeping machines, nurses moving quietly, and families whispering prayers of hope. My wife, Sherrie, underwent brain surgery with advanced laser technology, prompting me to reflect on the importance of focus—its influence not just on medical results but on the core of our lives._
__
**[Heidi Ganahl and Tori Stork end the “DougCo Dirty Dozen” with a warning to voters](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-mQ/s1s54877f6b)**
**BY RMV STAFF | LOCAL | NOVEMBER 4** _If Colorado’s education battles mirror the nation’s divide, Douglas County may be the front line. After two weeks and twelve hard-hitting episodes, Heidi Ganahl and her daughter, Tori Stork (formerly Ganahl), have made their case through the “Douglas County Dirty Dozen” video series: the state’s most conservative district is facing a coordinated push from national unions and progressive networks intent on steering local classrooms away from parents and community values._
__
**[When Democrats steal political yard signs, are they “protecting our democracy”?](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-mQ/s3sadab37d7)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-mQ/sbk452d2455)****BY RUSS ANDREWS | COMMENTARY | NOVEMBER 4** _Two local, moderate women are running for the Roaring Fork School District Board with their campaigns focused on the three R’s (reading, writing, and arithmetic). Their opponents are running their campaigns based on DEI (division, entitlement, and illiteracy)._
__

**Feel like you have a unique story, commentary or tip to share?** Email [submit@rockymountainvoice.com](mailto:submit@rockymountainvoice.com).

clean_body: Inside the 2025 Colorado elections: What voters rewarded, rejected—and why it matters | | | | PRESENTED BY | | Hey folks, We skipped our usual Mountain Minute yesterday because we spent the day breaking down key races, turnout trend, and voter shift across Colorado — so you wouldn’t have to. Here’s the quick version... Coloradans showed they’re still kinda paying attention. Voters rewarded engagement and punished drift, with turnout around 43% statewide. Conservatives notched a few wins, but the left’s union-backed organizing machine was more coordinated — and better funded — than ever. Read our full breakdown below or. Listen to today's Mountain Minute podcast , or watch on or . Here is a summary of today's top stories: 🏔️ Here in Colorado... Denver while critics warned it deepens debt and centralizes power. Meanwhile, 40+ counties led by Mesa County’s Bobbie Daniel are fighting that cost local taxpayers millions. In Montrose County, , recalled Commissioner Scott Mijares, and rejected new spending measures. And statewide, AG Phil Weiser’s to “Trump-proof” Colorado drew sharp criticism as a partisan power grab. 🇺🇸 On the National Stage... America’s divide deepened as in Virginia and New Jersey, where Democrats flipped school boards, legislatures, and local offices—driven by high blue-state turnout and backlash over federal dysfunction. In New York City, leaked documents showed the Democratic Socialists of America preparing to pressure incoming socialist mayor Zohran Mamdani to push an anti-Israel, anti-capitalist agenda—underscoring how heading into 2026. 🗣️ Commentary Spotlight... Bobbie Daniel’s rebukes the state’s unfunded mandates, likening them to bad business and urging lawmakers to “fund it or fix it.” Pastor Drake Hunter’s offers a spiritual reminder that what Coloradans focus on—truth or division—will define the state’s future. Together, their message is clear: Colorado’s renewal depends on . This is the Mountain Minute for Thursday, November 6, 2025. – Team RMV | TODAY'S TOP STORIES BY RMV EDITORIAL BOARD, Colorado voters made their message plain this year, though not all spoke with one voice. In city halls and school races they favored those who stayed engaged, turned away those who coasted and reminded every leader that trust has an expiration date. The RMV editorial board concludes that 2025 revealed both the strength of the left’s organization and the right’s fragmentation—union-backed PACs outspent grassroots efforts ten to one, flipping long-held conservative regions like Douglas and Mesa counties. The lesson heading into 2026, RMV warns, is one of unity and urgency: conservatives must rebuild infrastructure, align message and money, and fight on the terrain of meaning—not just policy—if they hope to compete in a Colorado increasingly shaped by emotional, organized, and progressive activism. | BY MICHAEL J BADAGLIACCO "MJB", Conservatives sweep Montrose County school board races: Voters backed candidates Neisha Balleck, Scott Scarborough, Tiffany Vincent, and Shane Daly—each campaigning on restoring academics, parental rights, and fiscal discipline—marking a decisive rejection of progressive education policies and “woke” agendas. Commissioner Scott Mijares recalled amid “dark money” influence: Mijares was ousted 52.5% to 47.5%, replaced by unaffiliated candidate Kirstin Copeland. The recall, driven more by personality conflicts than policy, highlighted growing tension between leadership style and community expectations in conservative Montrose. Fiscal restraint defines local ballot results: Voters rejected the Montrose City Charter Amendment and Library District Measures 6A and 6B, signaling resistance to higher taxes or expanded government spending and reaffirming a community-wide demand for accountability and limited government. | The Rocky Mountain Voice is a 501(c)(3) that exists to promote informed civic engagement and the principles of self-governance through enterprise reporting on public policy, politics, government, and culture throughout the state of Colorado. The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support! | Weiser backs mid-cycle redistricting: Colorado Attorney General Phil Weiser became the first statewide official—and a declared gubernatorial candidate—to support a constitutional amendment allowing Colorado to redraw congressional maps mid-decade to “Trump-proof” the state if GOP-led states gerrymander their maps. Proposed shift from Amendment Y’s safeguards: The 2018 voter-approved Amendment Y established a bipartisan 12-member commission to prevent partisan gerrymandering, but Weiser’s proposal would override that system, enabling Democrats to redraw districts before 2028. Critics call it a partisan power grab: Former House Speaker Frank McNulty denounced the plan as “a raw power play,” arguing Democrats are trying to secure more congressional seats for political gain amid fears of losing ground nationally. | ONLY ON ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE ⤵️ BY DRAKE HUNTER | COMMENTARY | NOVEMBER 5 While sitting in the waiting area at MD Anderson Cancer Center in Houston, I heard the characteristic sounds of hospital life—beeping machines, nurses moving quietly, and families whispering prayers of hope. My wife, Sherrie, underwent brain surgery with advanced laser technology, prompting me to reflect on the importance of focus—its influence not just on medical results but on the core of our lives. BY RMV STAFF | LOCAL | NOVEMBER 4 If Colorado’s education battles mirror the nation’s divide, Douglas County may be the front line. After two weeks and twelve hard-hitting episodes, Heidi Ganahl and her daughter, Tori Stork (formerly Ganahl), have made their case through the “Douglas County Dirty Dozen” video series: the state’s most conservative district is facing a coordinated push from national unions and progressive networks intent on steering local classrooms away from parents and community values. RUSS ANDREWS | COMMENTARY | NOVEMBER 4 Two local, moderate women are running for the Roaring Fork School District Board with their campaigns focused on the three R’s (reading, writing, and arithmetic). Their opponents are running their campaigns based on DEI (division, entitlement, and illiteracy). __ Feel like you have a unique story, commentary or tip to share? body:
[ stdin# ]Our Revolution Logo

Peter Willis,

Earlier this year, Zohran Mamdani was polling at 1%. This week, he won an
overwhelming and inspiring victory against the establishment and the far
right – and Our Revolution organizers and volunteers made half a MILLION
calls, texts, and door knocks to help elect him.

This surge of momentum didn’t stop in New York. We were able to use the
same strategies and tactics to win progressive victories up and down the
ballot across the country – proving that when candidates from the working
class offer real solutions and inspire voters, we WIN. And now we can’t
waste a SECOND in channeling this momentum towards the 2026 Midterms.

Our next step is our first 2026 Organizing Call on Monday, when we’ll hear
from an AMAZING slate of exciting candidates for 2026 – the next Mamdanis
– on how to build on this momentum, organize and WIN:

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34616?source=email-20251106&t=1011&akid=9792%2E5885482%2E-aFKvt ][IMG]

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34616?source=email-20251106&t=1012&akid=9792%2E5885482%2E-aFKvt ]RSVP Now to Join The Call to Get Ready for 2026!

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34616?source=email-20251106&t=1013&akid=9792%2E5885482%2E-aFKvt ]RSVP NOW

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/32994?source=email-20251106&t=1014&akid=9792%2E5885482%2E-aFKvt ]And if you can’t make it, donate $15 or whatever you can to boost our
organizing and power our victories in 2026!

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/32994?source=email-20251106&t=1015&akid=9792%2E5885482%2E-aFKvt ]Donate

We have one year until the midterms and we need to get to work NOW. So on
Monday, join us to hear from an amazing lineup, including:

* Graham Platner, a marine veteran and oysterman running to unseat Susan
Collins and fight for the working class in Maine.
* Robert Peters, who is challenging the establishment and running for
Congress in Illinois.
* Mai Vang, a progressive city council member backed by Our Revolution
in Sacramento, who is now running for Congress.
* Randy Bryce, a former ironworker who is running for Congress to unseat
a Republican incumbent in Wisconsin, and;
* Cori Bush, a longtime Our Revolution ally and progressive champion,
who is standing up again to win back her seat in Congress in Missouri.

Here’s the truth: Mamdani started with almost nothing except hope, grit,
and a refusal to back down. He spent months knocking on doors, leading
voter canvasses block by block, and pouring energy into real conversations
with voters across the city – all while Big Money and the establishment
threw everything they had against him.

Those same forces will do whatever it takes to try to stop us in 2026. But
we know what works – grassroots organizing, people-powered movements, and
a community that refuses to quit. That’s how we’ll flip vital
congressional seats, elect more working-class champions like Mamdani, and
take our country back from Trump and the Oligarchs.

Let’s keep building what we started this week. [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34616?source=email-20251106&t=1016&akid=9792%2E5885482%2E-aFKvt ]RSVP for Monday’s call
now – because when we organize, we win!!

Our Revolution

clean_body: [ stdin# ]Our Revolution Logo Peter Willis, Earlier this year, Zohran Mamdani was polling at 1%. This week, he won an overwhelming and inspiring victory against the establishment and the far right – and Our Revolution organizers and volunteers made half a MILLION calls, texts, and door knocks to help elect him. This surge of momentum didn’t stop in New York. We were able to use the same strategies and tactics to win progressive victories up and down the ballot across the country – proving that when candidates from the working class offer real solutions and inspire voters, we WIN. And now we can’t waste a SECOND in channeling this momentum towards the 2026 Midterms. Our next step is our first 2026 Organizing Call on Monday, when we’ll hear from an AMAZING slate of exciting candidates for 2026 – the next Mamdanis – on how to build on this momentum, organize and WIN: [ ][IMG] [ ]RSVP Now to Join The Call to Get Ready for 2026! [ ]RSVP NOW [ ]And if you can’t make it, donate $15 or whatever you can to boost our organizing and power our victories in 2026! [ ]Donate We have one year until the midterms and we need to get to work NOW. So on Monday, join us to hear from an amazing lineup, including: Graham Platner, a marine veteran and oysterman running to unseat Susan Collins and fight for the working class in Maine. Robert Peters, who is challenging the establishment and running for Congress in Illinois. Mai Vang, a progressive city council member backed by Our Revolution in Sacramento, who is now running for Congress. Randy Bryce, a former ironworker who is running for Congress to unseat a Republican incumbent in Wisconsin, and; * Cori Bush, a longtime Our Revolution ally and progressive champion, who is standing up again to win back her seat in Congress in Missouri. Here’s the truth: Mamdani started with almost nothing except hope, grit, and a refusal to back down. He spent months knocking on doors, leading voter canvasses block by block, and pouring energy into real conversations with voters across the city – all while Big Money and the establishment threw everything they had against him. Those same forces will do whatever it takes to try to stop us in 2026. But we know what works – grassroots organizing, people-powered movements, and a community that refuses to quit. That’s how we’ll flip vital congressional seats, elect more working-class champions like Mamdani, and take our country back from Trump and the Oligarchs. Let’s keep building what we started this week. [ ]RSVP for Monday’s call now – because when we organize, we win!! Our Revolution [ ]Facebook | [ ]Twitter | [ ]Instagram | [ ]Youtube | [ ]Website | [ ]Sign Up [ ]Our membership swag Our Revolution is the largest entirely grassroots funded progressive group in America.
