THEN → They kicked off a national redistricting war.GERRYMANDERING the maps in states like Texas, Indiana, and Missouri to cheat their way into a permanent majority. NOW → With Speaker Mike Johnson STILL refusing to swear in Democratic Rep.-elect Adelita Grijalva for OVER A MONTH , it’s clear to me that the Republican party will do ANYTHING to retain control over Washington. But I won’t stand for it. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = Thank you, Andrea | This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a key way for us to stay in touch and make sure you get the latest updates from our campaign. name: Wes Moore
subject: In Maryland, we protect our people
body:
# [](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/8?t=1&akid=885%2E1086043%2EVZZMu5)
Peter, in Maryland, _we protect our people._
With each passing day our government isn’t open, more Maryland families are feeling the strain – our people are scared, anxious, and unsure about what comes next.
But when I ran, I made one promise to Marylanders: **Leave No One Behind.**
No matter the challenge, I’ve kept that promise – launching relief for furloughed workers, protecting food assistance, and helping families keep their health coverage.
That’s what leadership looks like when Washington fails to act: **Compassion backed by action.** But sustaining this leadership takes all of us.
**[If you can, consider chipping in $10 to help us keep delivering for Maryland families during this shutdown. Whatever you chip in will help me fight for all of us.](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20251106-1.IvMwc_&refcode=em-20251106-1&t=4&akid=885%2E1086043%2EVZZMu5)**
Thank you.
Elevate,
Wes
[](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20251106-1.IvMwc_&refcode=em-20251106-1&t=5&akid=885%2E1086043%2EVZZMu5)

* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-footer?refcode=em-20251106-1&t=6&refcode2=885_1086043_VZZMu5&akid=885%2E1086043%2EVZZMu5)**

Email us: [info@wesmoore.com](mailto:info@wesmoore.com)
BY AUTHORITY: WES MOORE FOR MARYLAND, MARY TYDINGS, TREASURER

name: Team Kelly
subject: We are humbly asking you to personally endorse Mark Kelly
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
body:
From the Navy to NASA to the U.S. Senate, Mark has accomplished a lot. But if you ask him about any of it, here’s what he’ll tell you: “I never did it alone.”
#

Team — in just a moment, we’re going to ask you to sign on to personally endorse Mark Kelly.
Please let us explain why this small action is so important, especially today.
From the Navy to NASA to the U.S. Senate, Mark has accomplished a lot. But if you ask him about any of it, here’s what he’ll tell you: “I never did it alone.”
In the Navy, Mark had the crew of an entire aircraft carrier supporting his mission. At NASA, he had engineers, ground crew, technicians, and other astronauts with him. And in each of his campaigns, he would not have won without help from literally millions of people in Arizona and across the country.
And now, as Mark stands up to Donald Trump and his Administration, he needs your help again. That’s why we’re asking you to personally endorse Mark. It’s a small, simple action, but it speaks volumes about the strength of this team.
[**So, please. If you still have Mark’s back, will you please add your name and personally endorse him today? It’d mean a lot to us, and to Mark.**](https://act.markkelly.com/signup/mek-em-nov25-endorse/?source=em251106-1-8&akid=6238%2E6688874%2ERHyn0a)
**[ADD YOUR NAME](https://act.markkelly.com/signup/mek-em-nov25-endorse/?source=em251106-1-8&akid=6238%2E6688874%2ERHyn0a)**
Thank you so much,
Team Kelly

Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a very important way we communicate with supporters like you, but if you want to, you can [ unsubscribe here](https://act.markkelly.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=1&akid=6238%2E6688874%2ERHyn0a).
Mark Kelly was a Captain in the U.S. Navy and astronaut. Use of his military rank, job titles and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or Department of Defense and reference to NASA does not imply endorsement by the National Aeronautics and Space Administration.

name: MAHA Action
subject: Your Can’t-Miss Event Lineup, Virtual & In-Person
body:
>From discussions on wholeness to genetics to children's health defense, here's opportunities to connect.

| | | [ ](https://www.mahaaction.com/?e=674c9b4e7ea0bed829630c80c355f23d&utm_source=maha&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=event_round_up_251106_event_r3&n=1)
---
---
# Your Can’t-Miss Event Lineup
We're excited to share ways you can come together with others who care about health, freedom, and a better future. Whether you join in person or online, don’t miss the opportunity to connect, learn, and take action alongside our growing movement.
---
### **Children’s Health Defense 2025 Annual Conference
****November 7–9 | Austin, TX**
---
[ ](https://www.mahaaction.com/r?u=PypJnAvZJfMID82XYaEoEK5YTwNTAJNbbI7wcCRWzMEr-KzIafaNeJpQbsvll-RxxVJ5-JdZiJccrmsxc99qTw&e=674c9b4e7ea0bed829630c80c355f23d&utm_source=maha&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=event_round_up_251106_event_r3&n=2)
---
Join **Senator Rand Paul** , **Dr. Joseph Ladapo** , **Russell Brand** , **Dr. Peter McCullough** , and other leading voices for a weekend of truth, courage, and solutions. Together, they’ll unpack the lessons of the past five years and chart the future of informed consent, parental rights, and medical freedom. This is where the next chapter of the health freedom movement begins—bold ideas, real science, and united action in Austin, TX. **[Learn more and register →](https://www.mahaaction.com/r?u=PypJnAvZJfMID82XYaEoEK5YTwNTAJNbbI7wcCRWzMEr-KzIafaNeJpQbsvll-RxxVJ5-JdZiJccrmsxc99qTw&e=674c9b4e7ea0bed829630c80c355f23d&utm_source=maha&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=event_round_up_251106_event_r3&n=3)**
---
### **Eudemonia Summit
****November 13–16 | West Palm Beach, FL**
A three-day gathering to transform your health, led by visionaries charting the future of wellness, longevity, and human potential. With guests like **Andrew Huberman** , **Gabby Bernstein** , **Mark Hyman** , and much more, it's surely to be a can't-miss opportunity. **[Learn more and register →](https://www.mahaaction.com/r?u=0ZF8-FzeoMGkg19O2WpiLNX3rb-DTG6f947IfRayzlk&e=674c9b4e7ea0bed829630c80c355f23d&utm_source=maha&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=event_round_up_251106_event_r3&n=4)**
---
### MAHA Institute presents _Reclaiming Science: The People's NIH with Dr. Jay Bhattacharya
_ November 19 | Washington, DC
---
[ ](https://www.mahaaction.com/r?u=YmOSYJYZ5T0wUQpBKrzpY9eHdUq7piKHqNZXFTTWyhEmQovm448PgiYDQvh8cgDGLk5tph1rkyWnZZCh1hnrkA&e=674c9b4e7ea0bed829630c80c355f23d&utm_source=maha&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=event_round_up_251106_event_r3&n=5)
---
Join MAHA Institute for the Reclaiming Science Round Table: The People’s NIH, a vital conversation with NIH Director Dr. Jay Bhattacharya and leading scientists from across our federal health agencies. Space is limited. Register for this in-person event using the invitation code "ACTION". **[Learn more and register →](https://www.mahaaction.com/r?u=YmOSYJYZ5T0wUQpBKrzpY9eHdUq7piKHqNZXFTTWyhEmQovm448PgiYDQvh8cgDGLk5tph1rkyWnZZCh1hnrkA&e=674c9b4e7ea0bed829630c80c355f23d&utm_source=maha&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=event_round_up_251106_event_r3&n=6)
******
---
### The Future of Mental Health in America
December 3–4 | West Hartford, CT
Beginning with an evening cocktail reception on Wednesday, December 3, we’re bringing together current and former patients, clinicians, researchers, policymakers, journalists, academics, family members, and advocates to take a hard look at today’s mental health industry, explore future opportunities for change, and reimagine the ways we understand ourselves and the challenges that come with being human. **[Learn more and register →](https://www.mahaaction.com/r?u=ctq-YvbWa8kWswGAIubPxAN_zYTAnGHkcP7EgPdyyFXD6PLvA1qTAw7ckjKYN09h&e=674c9b4e7ea0bed829630c80c355f23d&utm_source=maha&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=event_round_up_251106_event_r3&n=7)**
---
---
---
| [ ](https://www.mahaaction.com/r?u=PWPiYaxM73AUiFXZdpyaD6J06ilu2zt1fAqVrE3TX3L5aRgcSsKvHg9e72m6iL-NTAH8zpO9HgvFujU19Sqz2Q&e=674c9b4e7ea0bed829630c80c355f23d&utm_source=maha&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=event_round_up_251106_event_r3&n=8)

We dropped a limited-edition drop of cozy essentials made to help you gift with purpose. disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bera for Congress
body:
I have some big news...

| Peter — _I have some big news..._

Shortly after Proposition 50 passed on Tuesday, I announced my re-election campaign in California’s new 3rd Congressional District!

Before I say more: Winning this seat is absolutely critical to taking back the House in 2026 – and we can’t afford to fall short on day one. My team has set a goal to raise $9,891 before midnight. [**Will you be one of the first people to donate to my campaign for CA-03?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118805145/580951155/762360330?amount=8&source=EM_NBI_AB_20251106_FR_NewDistrict_0_12MO_AmiBera_NA_NA_NA&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS85MDU3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhNmVmOGNiMy01OWJiLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRkNmYzMzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=34hQJS6VSsx4HXiXZVwunApm3dSGMHmnemYpM6hvH0o=) | | [DONATE ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118805147/580951157/768768638?amount=8&source=EM_NBI_AB_20251106_FR_NewDistrict_0_12MO_AmiBera_NA_NA_NA&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS85MDU3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhNmVmOGNiMy01OWJiLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRkNmYzMzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=34hQJS6VSsx4HXiXZVwunApm3dSGMHmnemYpM6hvH0o=)

Shortly after Proposition 50 passed on Tuesday, I announced my re-election campaign in California’s new 3rd Congressional District! Before I say more: Winning this seat is absolutely critical to taking back the House in 2026 – and we can’t afford to fall short on day one. My team has set a goal to raise $9,891 before midnight. = | | = It’s been just over 13 years since I was first elected to Congress, and since then we’ve accomplished so much together. name: Scott Peters
subject: We need to act now.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Scott Peters for Congress
body:
---
| | Peter,

California made history this week. **Voters passed Prop 50 and secured a major victory for fair representation and real democracy.** That is something to celebrate, and it happened because people like you stood up and showed up.

**But here is what it means for us: Prop 50 will make California’s 50th District more competitive.** National groups and outside spending organizations are already signaling interest. They see this as an opportunity to flip a seat. We need to make sure they are wrong.

I have worked every day to support San Diego’s innovation economy, military families, affordable housing solutions, and clean energy jobs for our community. I am proud of that work, and I’m committed to continuing it. But to stay ahead, we need to start preparing now.

That means expanding our voter outreach in newly drawn areas, building our campaign resources before the attacks start, and growing a stronger grassroots base that can hold firm when the race tightens. **This is not the moment to wait. This is the moment to show strength.**

[Can you make a contribution today to help us build the foundation we need to win? Every donation right now directly supports our voter contact efforts and early organizing, which will be critical as the map shifts.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118805444/580952073/418324714?amount=10&refcode2=5cb1d1b3-5abb-f011-8e61-6045bdd6f338&refcode=SP_EM_FR_180DO_251106_1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzkvMS84ODA2MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1Y2IxZDFiMy01YWJiLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRkNmYzMzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=r1AKQeSLlSHTj0Ww4c2U2VUdNlTxRc5l0sdXe2rzgM4=) | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately by clicking a button below. clean_body: | | Peter, California made history this week. Voters passed Prop 50 and secured a major victory for fair representation and real democracy. That is something to celebrate, and it happened because people like you stood up and showed up. But here is what it means for us: Prop 50 will make California’s 50th District more competitive. National groups and outside spending organizations are already signaling interest. They see this as an opportunity to flip a seat. We need to make sure they are wrong. I have worked every day to support San Diego’s innovation economy, military families, affordable housing solutions, and clean energy jobs for our community. I am proud of that work, and I’m committed to continuing it. But to stay ahead, we need to start preparing now. That means expanding our voter outreach in newly drawn areas, building our campaign resources before the attacks start, and growing a stronger grassroots base that can hold firm when the race tightens. | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.
---

The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. name: Kirk Cameron
subject: Bring wholesome messaging back to children’s entertainment
body:
Please - for the sake of all our families

| | | You are absolutely right if you think Hollywood is reaching right into your family living room with "entertainment" that grooms children.
---
| |
---
| | Dear Friend in Faith, Please - for the sake of all our families - take it from me as someone who's lived and worked in Hollywood my whole life... You are absolutely right if you think Hollywood is reaching right into your family living room with "entertainment" that grooms children. You may remember shows with wholesome messages like Father Knows Best, Marcus Welby, M.D., and even Dennis the Menace. But today when children turn on the TV, they see a Drag Queen leading the Pride Parade on _Blue 's Clues_...
---
[](https://email.editor.conservativebroadcast.net/c/eJyUkc2q2zAQhZ9G3ilIox87Cy_i6zgUmlKySOlKSPLYEbWtICm5r18MvQ_Q7cfMge-cX1dztWEJ2_zDrth6Z1I2LoVt_nzEBXNcccWc7Ry22VkDDBTlnDK93_FqbP0RQTpZYctrDVJrKZvq0TZe8UYp0DBN2gPUyBxvlJZHMTE_-iq0exbnTAMwJpoDyEkKYbViVvlReCIZjqHEdPBxy5jetoQ3uhTt6G0uhw1LtbSPUp6ZiBOBgcCQ0b8SHuyGYywHH1cCg0v2jdTF-CcTGKDmCjw0dFSoqXRS0obrhqJytdZYT82REzG8ympyfCWPRPRdf6G3852A3vGKY3itRPTn6z_i7fq0Yd6I6HcnJjkzXX8x3e10P5tvl8tvUN_N7Xw3P0_0o_v6ilvBrRDRf3T0q4sqtePzMyxLyLomks2rDcsuUpX_3ubdwt8AAAD__w0hlWQ)
---
| | Or Sesame Street using YOUR tax-dollars to normalize same-sex parents and men in dresses. And the result is an upside-down world where children lecture YOU about what you can and cannot say... ...and they try to "educate" YOU about your "old-fashioned" and "mean" ideas. Today you have a very special chance to stop the Hollywood groomers who are turning our children and grandchildren against us and the Godly values we love. **[Today, I'm inviting you to be a Founding Supporter of the very first children's television show that actively fights back against the groomers in Hollywood AND reinforces the traditional biblical values that made America great.](https://email.editor.conservativebroadcast.net/c/eJyUkc2q2zAQhZ9G3ilIox87Cy_i6zgUmlKySOlKSPLYEbWtICm5r18MvQ_Q7cfMge-cX1dztWEJ2_zDrth6Z1I2LoVt_nzEBXNcccWc7Ry22VkDDBTlnDK93_FqbP0RQTpZYctrDVJrKZvq0TZe8UYp0DBN2gPUyBxvlJZHMTE_-iq0exbnTAMwJpoDyEkKYbViVvlReCIZjqHEdPBxy5jetoQ3uhTt6G0uhw1LtbSPUp6ZiBOBgcCQ0b8SHuyGYywHH1cCg0v2jdTF-CcTGKDmCjw0dFSoqXRS0obrhqJytdZYT82REzG8ympyfCWPRPRdf6G3852A3vGKY3itRPTn6z_i7fq0Yd6I6HcnJjkzXX8x3e10P5tvl8tvUN_N7Xw3P0_0o_v6ilvBrRDRf3T0q4sqtePzMyxLyLomks2rDcsuUpX_3ubdwt8AAAD__w0hlWQ)** Introducing: _Iggy and Mr. Kirk_. I'm making this ground-breaking live-action television show with the great folks at **Brave Books**. If you've seen me and **Brave Books** on _Fox News_ , you know we've already countered those awful "Drag Queen Story Hours" the Left has been pushing on children in our taxpayer funded libraries. Thanks to support from parents, grandparents, and concerned citizens just like you, we started hosting Godly and patriotic "Story Hours" of our own. And now, THOUSANDS of children and parents can gather to pledge allegiance to our flag, read wholesome books, and pray together in their libraries. It's encouraging to see we can win when we fight back! Now we're working to have that same impact in television.
---
>> Imagine a children's show that leaves your child or grandchild with a greater sense of right and wrong and an understanding of truth. Courage, honesty, discernment, forgiveness, empathy, all taught through a biblical and traditional lens. >> Imagine a children's show that brings families together instead of driving them apart. >> Now imagine YOU are the one who makes that show possible. Because we can't beat the Hollywood groomers without your help as a **Founding Supporter**.
---
The great news is that we've already completed and released the first ten character-building episodes of _Iggy and Mr. Kirk_! We premiered it recently to a small group of children and parents and I wish you could have seen the smiles on those innocent little faces. And I wish you could have experienced _the parents_ watching their children absorb the beautiful Gospel messages. It was quite a moment for everyone - including folks just like you - who worked so hard to get us that far. But now **Brave Books** has an urgent two-fold problem that I hope you can help with.
---
_**#1. Brave Books needs to launch a massive advertising blitz to get around the groomers on the Left.**_
---
You see, we've made the first five episodes of Season 1 of _Iggy and Mr. Kirk_ available absolutely FREE. That's the best way we can reach MILLIONS of families with our Gospel-inspired messaging. But no one will see it if we can't get the word out. And the Left will try to bury _Iggy and Mr. Kirk_ at every turn. The price tag for an advertising blitz of this magnitude is $250,000 - and with the recent launch, we need to raise the funds immediately.
---
_**#2. We must complete Season 2 to keep our momentum going and that cost is $250,000 for post production.**_
---
I know that's a lot of money but it's actually a fraction of what it would cost if we made our show with a major Hollywood studio. And obviously, we won't work through Hollywood. name: Forward Party
subject: History Being Made - Forward Party Election Wins and Big News in Arizona
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Forward Party and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
Here's what's happening in the Forward world...

| | [ ](https://home.forwardparty.com/r?u=JgyyvCfQwldlSeD7KJl_Ih7269o_4hwqOhPs1_PHvgI&e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fast_forward_84a&n=1)
---
Thursday, November 06, 2025 | Peter -- It’s hard to believe we’re now entering the fifth week of the government shutdown – a stark reminder of why Forward exists. As Washington grinds to a halt, our movement is proving that cooperation and competition _can_ coexist and that Americans are ready for a better way. Tuesday’s results sent a clear message: when voters are given a real choice, they take it. Forward Party candidates won offices across the country — from mayors to sheriffs to school boards — proving that communities are ready to elect leaders who put people over party. These aren’t symbolic campaigns. These are real wins, real seats, and real groundwork for 2026.
### **Historic Wins**
**Jay Doyle — Mayor-elect of Georgetown, South Carolina**
* Elected _on the Forward Party ballot line_ with 56% of the vote. First Forward Party ballot line candidate elected in SC

* Possibly the first third-party candidate to win a partisan election in South Carolina since 1894 (_As far as we can tell)_.

* A breakthrough moment showing that Forward candidates can win outright, not just compete.

**Danny Ceisler — Bucks County Sheriff-elect, Pennsylvania**
* Forward-endorsed and elected with 55.28% of the vote.

* Defeated the incumbent in one of Pennsylvania’s most closely watched local races.

* Demonstrates that Forward candidates can win in competitive, suburban counties that will matter in 2026.

### **More Forward Victories Nationwide**
****
### **On To 2026**
### These wins aren’t the finish line — they’re the launchpad. Here's why they matter for the 2026 midterms:
* **Proof of concept:** Forward candidates are winning outright — not as protest votes, but with majorities.

* **Ballot lines are real and growing:** South Carolina, Pennsylvania, Connecticut, and more states now have Forward-elected officials, making full party status and ballot access easier for 2026 congressional and state legislative races.

* Jessica Forsell, Eva McGowen, and Zach Thompson in PA all ran and won on the FWD ballot line

* **Candidate pipeline for 2026:** The mayors, council members, sheriffs, judges, and finance board members elected this week are the people who can recruit, train, and endorse the next wave of Forward candidates — including for statehouse and congressional seats.

* **Credibility with donors and media:** Success turns curiosity into investment. These wins make it easier to raise funds, attract talent, and be taken seriously as a national political contender.

* **Model for victory:** Local-focused, community-first candidates + coalition voters (independents, moderate Rs and Ds) = a roadmap for 2026 swing districts.

### **Forward + Arizona Independent Party Partnership**
We’re thrilled to share that Forward has entered into a formal cooperation agreement with the newly formed Arizona Independent Party (AIP): a partnership that creates a powerful pathway for independent candidates to run and win in 2026 and beyond. The AIP, founded and chaired by former Phoenix Mayor Paul Johnson, is a renaming of the Arizona No Labels Party and provides an existing ballot line and the infrastructure independents have long lacked. Together, we are preserving and expanding that access by ensuring independent voices can compete in local, state, and federal races. As Lindsey said in today’s press release: _“Independent voters are the fastest-growing force in Arizona, and they deserve more than a broken two-party system that doesn’t speak for them. What’s happening here is part of a national movement to empower independents across the country and reshape American politics for the better.”_ You can read the full announcement [here](https://www.forwardparty.com/media/blog/newly-formed-arizona-independent-party-joins-with-national-forward-party-to-create-pathway-for-independent-candidates-to-run-for-office/?e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fast_forward_84a&n=2). **Forward on Prop 50** We developed our statement on California's Proposition 50 after weighing multiple perspectives within the Forward movement and acknowledging the real dilemma voters faced. We wanted to recognize both the anti-democratic gerrymandering happening in states like Texas and North Carolina and the fact that many California voters supported Prop 50 as a defensive move, not because they opposed independent redistricting. Ultimately, we crafted language that is honest about the partisan escalation while staying true to Forward’s core belief that voters—not politicians—should draw the lines and choose their leaders.
* * *
**[Georgetown elects Forward Party mayor in city first](https://home.forwardparty.com/r?u=MNU_jJg0xO2zMsP4lMO9fivvrvooHXfJhuzg5V5JIL7hc3wbWkiQZg9B6VMcPRrNTHvad5C3z5C0EQQfep1rwGZ1F8Gu_0KGmsLu9tQjnbZlhqTt8TW2JB0O-rRqWvJ6&e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fast_forward_84a&n=3) | News2** Doyle called his win “A victory for the people,” saying it reflects residents’ desire for bold change and a renewed focus on community needs. **[Democrat Danny Ceisler ousts Fred Harran in Bucks County sheriff's race](https://home.forwardparty.com/r?u=RvmuK9_akypAjclIVyPol7OzJlrh8RN1EeGphGbMi3pdJb-KVIqU30VvWduiNMHe9EuAMfW85vS0C1vNgZzvqCwZQpMn93diKCqEMWbAw3I&e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fast_forward_84a&n=4) | WHYY** Ceisler, 33, a U.S. Army veteran, moved to Bristol Borough in Bucks County in 2020. He told WHYY News that as sheriff, he plans to “take politics out of law enforcement, focus on nuts and bolts public safety, and end the extreme and ridiculous partnership with” U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement.
**If you’re tired of waiting for the system to fix itself — this is your moment.****
**👥[ Volunteer ](https://home.forwardparty.com/your_dashboard?e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fast_forward_84a&n=5)| 🗳️ [Run for Office ](https://www.forwardparty.com/runforoffice/?e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fast_forward_84a&n=6)| 💸[ Donate](https://home.forwardparty.com/donate?e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fast_forward_84a&n=7) | 🌐 [forwardparty.com](https://home.forwardparty.com/r?u=jBnsgtW36mAL7APXvIBDIH9UBFQynkqNd5w2mIH3Wu4&e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fast_forward_84a&n=8)
[****](https://home.forwardparty.com/r?u=KsRbRlupkMENFsDlxp7flkcx8_xxQzkKAb7MgB1YMrByhnj4sje4BRVQM1-uGNHhgFi8N7bFbeE6G0P5bRxqrlqS-sG8Z0siEuI44bo4m-9-zr39gcVm7G_9hKi2QA_Yxu1cqhF01-zsM_s_xwFwMg&e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fast_forward_84a&n=9)[**California voters OK new congressional lines, boosting Democrats ahead of midterms**](https://home.forwardparty.com/r?u=8c4KZMXYR5lO6Tv9ZXupefhyGivg3xF3CRQOGA4OnCfFA2PF2GnftswyRSzb1AOUvHBIr6qOI04diLtEelQKj3dAgk0vTUauHdZfC2J5BAXeIovOfLlfYbThvKX1YxZnvlJZJKNVIH2nmJdTrrlsIg&e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fast_forward_84a&n=10)| **NPR** California voters have easily approved a ballot measure to redraw the state's congressional map to favor Democrats, according to a race call by The Associated Press, marking the party's biggest victory to date in a national battle over redistricting ahead of the 2026 midterms.
* * *

clean_body: Here's what's happening in the Forward world... | | Thursday, November 06, 2025 | Peter -- It’s hard to believe we’re now entering the fifth week of the government shutdown – a stark reminder of why Forward exists. As Washington grinds to a halt, our movement is proving that cooperation and competition can coexist and that Americans are ready for a better way. Tuesday’s results sent a clear message: when voters are given a real choice, they take it. Forward Party candidates won offices across the country — from mayors to sheriffs to school boards — proving that communities are ready to elect leaders who put people over party. These aren’t symbolic campaigns. These are real wins, real seats, and real groundwork for 2026. Historic Wins Jay Doyle — Mayor-elect of Georgetown, South Carolina Elected on the Forward Party ballot line with 56% of the vote. First Forward Party ballot line candidate elected in SC Possibly the first third-party candidate to win a partisan election in South Carolina since 1894 (As far as we can tell). A breakthrough moment showing that Forward candidates can win outright, not just compete. Danny Ceisler — Bucks County Sheriff-elect, Pennsylvania Forward-endorsed and elected with 55.28% of the vote. Defeated the incumbent in one of Pennsylvania’s most closely watched local races. Demonstrates that Forward candidates can win in competitive, suburban counties that will matter in 2026. More Forward Victories Nationwide ** On To 2026 These wins aren’t the finish line — they’re the launchpad. Here's why they matter for the 2026 midterms: Proof of concept: Forward candidates are winning outright — not as protest votes, but with majorities. Ballot lines are real and growing: South Carolina, Pennsylvania, Connecticut, and more states now have Forward-elected officials, making full party status and ballot access easier for 2026 congressional and state legislative races. Jessica Forsell, Eva McGowen, and Zach Thompson in PA all ran and won on the FWD ballot line Candidate pipeline for 2026: The mayors, council members, sheriffs, judges, and finance board members elected this week are the people who can recruit, train, and endorse the next wave of Forward candidates — including for statehouse and congressional seats. Credibility with donors and media: Success turns curiosity into investment. These wins make it easier to raise funds, attract talent, and be taken seriously as a national political contender. Model for victory: Local-focused, community-first candidates + coalition voters (independents, moderate Rs and Ds) = a roadmap for 2026 swing districts. Forward + Arizona Independent Party Partnership We’re thrilled to share that Forward has entered into a formal cooperation agreement with the newly formed Arizona Independent Party (AIP): a partnership that creates a powerful pathway for independent candidates to run and win in 2026 and beyond. The AIP, founded and chaired by former Phoenix Mayor Paul Johnson, is a renaming of the Arizona No Labels Party and provides an existing ballot line and the infrastructure independents have long lacked. Together, we are preserving and expanding that access by ensuring independent voices can compete in local, state, and federal races. As Lindsey said in today’s press release: “Independent voters are the fastest-growing force in Arizona, and they deserve more than a broken two-party system that doesn’t speak for them. What’s happening here is part of a national movement to empower independents across the country and reshape American politics for the better.” You can read the full announcement . Forward on Prop 50 We developed our statement on California's Proposition 50 after weighing multiple perspectives within the Forward movement and acknowledging the real dilemma voters faced. We wanted to recognize both the anti-democratic gerrymandering happening in states like Texas and North Carolina and the fact that many California voters supported Prop 50 as a defensive move, not because they opposed independent redistricting. Ultimately, we crafted language that is honest about the partisan escalation while staying true to Forward’s core belief that voters—not politicians—should draw the lines and choose their leaders. | News2 Doyle called his win “A victory for the people,” saying it reflects residents’ desire for bold change and a renewed focus on community needs. | WHYY Ceisler, 33, a U.S. Army veteran, moved to Bristol Borough in Bucks County in 2020. He told WHYY News that as sheriff, he plans to “take politics out of law enforcement, focus on nuts and bolts public safety, and end the extreme and ridiculous partnership with” U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement. If you’re tired of waiting for the system to fix itself — this is your moment. 👥| 🗳️ | 💸 | 🌐 | NPR California voters have easily approved a ballot measure to redraw the state's congressional map to favor Democrats, according to a race call by The Associated Press, marking the party's biggest victory to date in a national battle over redistricting ahead of the 2026 midterms. * Copyright © 2025 Forward Party, all rights reserved. body:
|
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118806018/580954200/-530215862?refcode=LOC_EM_PET_2025.11.06_OTS-Thank-Pelosi_B2_F1_S1_C1_90AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZGNhOTZmMS01YmJiLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRkNmYzMzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=inrO16pMckxeSKfdDvLitwjbqDhWPXtTCOWYYQ0peRg=)
Folks, For decades, Nancy Pelosi has shown the world what it means to lead with courage, conviction and heart. Today, she announced her retirement. **[_Will you join me and the Off the Sidelines community and sign our card thanking her for her nearly forty years of service?_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118806022/580954206/1495181258?refcode=LOC_EM_PET_2025.11.06_OTS-Thank-Pelosi_B2_F1_S1_C1_90AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZGNhOTZmMS01YmJiLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRkNmYzMzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=inrO16pMckxeSKfdDvLitwjbqDhWPXtTCOWYYQ0peRg=&emci=0b47e4e6-3fbb-f011-8e61-6045bdd6f338&emdi=2dca96f1-5bbb-f011-8e61-6045bdd6f338&ceid=7659029)** She shattered glass ceilings as the first woman ever elected Speaker of the House. She’s been an extraordinary force — fighting for working families, defending democracy and never backing down in the face of challenges. And she’s done it all with grace and grit. Nancy Pelosi’s leadership has made history. She’s delivered landmark legislation, protected our fundamental rights and inspired generations of women and girls to step off the sidelines and into the arena. clean_body: | = Folks, For decades, Nancy Pelosi has shown the world what it means to lead with courage, conviction and heart. Today, she announced her retirement. She shattered glass ceilings as the first woman ever elected Speaker of the House. She’s been an extraordinary force — fighting for working families, defending democracy and never backing down in the face of challenges. And she’s done it all with grace and grit. Nancy Pelosi’s leadership has made history. She’s delivered landmark legislation, protected our fundamental rights and inspired generations of women and girls to step off the sidelines and into the arena. Now, as she closes a remarkable chapter in her public service, I want to make sure she knows just how much she’s meant to all of us. | | Let’s show her the gratitude she deserves for breaking barriers and getting things done. Thank you for joining me in celebrating this trailblazer and role model. Nancy’s legacy will continue to light the way for every woman who dares to lead. With appreciation, Kirsten Gillibrand Off the Sidelines © 2025 | Paid for by Off the Sidelines PAC

name: Lindsey Scholten
subject: Save the Date: OLCV’s Town Hall with Senator Wyden!
body:
https://secure.ngpvan.com/4E0z8mwxE0ahw93hgRTJ2w2?contactdata=0NHtcHtn0HRfqEHZwIMZTXvZMPPCYZJVEJvRVjzZY6RFrvVXow7gI82to6LiDlOjwKqS57UtoqRamFVflVzJIPy4htw5bn43O/qGJ5kSkfR/iqKS27YJ2rCDsip7WuaR [https://secure.ngpvan.com/4E0z8mwxE0ahw93hgRTJ2w2?contactdata=0NHtcHtn0HRfqEHZwIMZTXvZMPPCYZJVEJvRVjzZY6RFrvVXow7gI82to6LiDlOjwKqS57UtoqRamFVflVzJIPy4htw5bn43O/qGJ5kSkfR/iqKS27YJ2rCDsip7WuaR]
Dear Peter,
We’re excited to announce that on Friday, December 5th, OLCV will be holding a Town Hall on the Environment with Senator Ron Wyden. With the doom of the federal government shut down and ongoing federal threats against our environment, we need climate champions now more than ever. Senator Wyden has been a champion for clean energy tax credits, climate, and the environment. Don’t miss this special opportunity — save the date!
OLCV’s Town Hall on the Environment with Senator Ron Wyden
Save the Date for: Friday, December 5th
Via Zoom
Sign up and submit your questions here! [https://secure.ngpvan.com/4E0z8mwxE0ahw93hgRTJ2w2?contactdata=0NHtcHtn0HRfqEHZwIMZTXvZMPPCYZJVEJvRVjzZY6RFrvVXow7gI82to6LiDlOjwKqS57UtoqRamFVflVzJIPy4htw5bn43O/qGJ5kSkfR/iqKS27YJ2rCDsip7WuaR]
Senator Wyden will answer pre-selected questions from audience members that fit into the categories of: public lands, clean energy, climate, including Endangerment Finding, federal funding cuts to environmental programs and agencies, and other environmental issues. Attendees will have the opportunity to submit their questions, which you can do if you sign up here [https://secure.ngpvan.com/4E0z8mwxE0ahw93hgRTJ2w2] !
Now’s the time to mark your calendars so you don’t miss this!
Sincerely,
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/AV/AVLCV/1/70877/images/Lindsey.png]
Lindsey Scholten (she/her)
OLCV Executive Director
https://www.facebook.com/oregonlcv [https://www.facebook.com/oregonlcv] https://bsky.app/profile/olcv.org [https://bsky.app/profile/olcv.org] https://www.instagram.com/olcv/?next=%2F [https://www.instagram.com/olcv/?next=%2F] https://x.com/OLCV [https://x.com/OLCV]

Oregon League of Conservation Voters
1110 SE Alder Suite 220
Portland, OR 97214
United States

We can’t let him PRIVATIZE and DESTROY it — but we NEED your help!

[**As a top grassroots Democrat, will you sign our petition to stand with the USPS? **FACT #2:** The USPS recently confirmed that more than 10,000+ postal workers will be laid off — _**your local post office could be next!**_

**FACT #3:** This is Trump’s latest attack on YOUR right to vote — he’s going after the USPS and mail-in ballots because he wants to RIG the 2026 election!

[**THAT’S WHY WE NEED A SURGE OF 150 TOP DEMOCRATS TO STAND WITH USPS: Add your name by MIDNIGHT and defend your local Post Office from MAGA destruction! »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/save-usps/?source=VPP_20251106_EMPET_E3_T0_AC_RS&t=9&akid=21742%2E878179%2EAHiWjM)

clean_body: I won’t stay quiet while families are starving. | = I was outside of Speaker Mike Johnson’s office today because he hasn’t offered a single, tangible reason as to why he’s putting billionaire abusers over the needs of everyday people. So today, I gave Mike Johnson and other Republicans the chance to change my mind: Mike Johnson is starving families and gutting healthcare to cover up the Epstein Files. This week is the first week where SNAP and WIC recipients have lost access to aid as a result of the GOP shutdown — which Republicans are allowing to drag on to protect billionaires by gutting healthcare from the American people and distracting from the Epstein files. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = = | = = | = = Thanks for standing with me, Yassamin Yassamin Ansari for Congress PO Box 13524 Phoenix, AZ 85002 United States | mailto:info@yassaminforcongress.com | Paid for by Yassamin Ansari for Congress.

Friend, The loss of mobility and the resulting isolation can be devastating for someone. name: Larry Hailsham Jr., Executive Director
subject: we will build on this momentum
disclaimer_text: Paid for by The Pennsylvania Democratic Party
body:
| |
---
Peter,

On the first election night of the new era of the Pennsylvania Democratic Party, the voters of our Commonwealth delivered a resounding message by standing up for our freedoms and rejecting Trump’s chaos.

In the face of wealthy special interests pushing rampant misinformation and a dangerous agenda, Pennsylvania voters retained all three Supreme Court justices – protecting our freedoms and rejecting MAGA billionaires’ efforts to buy the Court.

Tuesday was a first step in building back a Pennsylvania Democratic Party that can win back voters we lost in 2024 by putting forward leaders who will work every day to get stuff done for them.

With the leadership of Governor Josh Shapiro, Lieutenant Governor Austin Davis, and Chairman DePasquale, the party is committed to building on this momentum and winning all across Pennsylvania in 2026.

Thank you so much for standing with us,

Larry Hailsham
Executive Director, Pennsylvania Democratic Party

clean_body: | | Peter, On the first election night of the new era of the Pennsylvania Democratic Party, the voters of our Commonwealth delivered a resounding message by standing up for our freedoms and rejecting Trump’s chaos. In the face of wealthy special interests pushing rampant misinformation and a dangerous agenda, Pennsylvania voters retained all three Supreme Court justices – protecting our freedoms and rejecting MAGA billionaires’ efforts to buy the Court. Tuesday was a first step in building back a Pennsylvania Democratic Party that can win back voters we lost in 2024 by putting forward leaders who will work every day to get stuff done for them. With the leadership of Governor Josh Shapiro, Lieutenant Governor Austin Davis, and Chairman DePasquale, the party is committed to building on this momentum and winning all across Pennsylvania in 2026. name: Saikat
subject: Nancy Pelosi’s retirement closes a historic chapter, but the next one is being written right now
body:
This is our moment to prove that politics can be powered by people again

| | | | | [|
---](https://saikat.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6ed3ace2702e5b912b1693cee&id=8906661293&e=bf17056ae8)
| | | | | | What a day.
Today, Speaker Emerita Nancy Pelosi announced that she will not seek re-election. It’s hard to overstate what that means – not just for San Francisco, but for American politics. For nearly forty years, Pelosi has been one of the most powerful figures in Washington. She’s made history as the first woman to serve as Speaker of the House. She’s guided the Democratic Party through some of the toughest fights of our time.
[**In a second, I’m going to ask you to donate to my campaign and own a piece of our movement**](https://saikat.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6ed3ace2702e5b912b1693cee&id=58ea41a68e&e=bf17056ae8), but first, I wanted to share my thoughts on how this isn’t just about the end of an era — it’s about what comes next.
For decades, San Francisco has been represented by someone at the very top of the political establishment: the center of power in a Democratic Party that’s become increasingly disconnected from the people it claims to represent. And that’s why this announcement matters. Because for the first time in nearly forty years, our city has a real chance to choose a different kind of leadership.
Leadership that doesn’t just manage the decline of a broken system, but rebuilds our city and our country.
Our campaign is about winning Medicare for All, building millions of affordable homes, kicking PG&E out of the district and establishing public power, and ending the era of billionaires and lobbyists buying our democracy. It’s about returning San Francisco to the values that once made it a beacon of innovation, community, and courage; not a playground for the ultra-wealthy while working families are priced out.
This is our moment to prove that politics can be powered by people again.
Already, over 2,000 volunteers have signed up to help us knock on doors across the city, and we’ve already knocked more than 50,000 doors. This is just the beginning. The political establishment will now rush to fill Pelosi’s seat with another career politician backed by corporate PACs and insider donors. But we’re building something different.
If you’re in San Francisco, we’d love you on the doors with us now. We have a ton of fun, with happy hours, picnics and volleyball afterwards. But most importantly, the most powerful thing you can do right now is talk to voters directly, because that’s how we win elections and change what’s possible in politics.
---
| [DONATE](https://saikat.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6ed3ace2702e5b912b1693cee&id=2351114575&e=bf17056ae8)
---
| |
Nancy Pelosi’s retirement closes a historic chapter, but the next one is being written right now. And if we do this right, it won’t be about one politician or one seat. It’ll be about a movement of people who decided to take back their democracy.
Let’s make history, San Francisco!
– Saikat
---
| | | | | | | | |

---
|
---

| This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail.com](mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com)
[_why did I get this?_](https://saikat.us14.list-manage.com/about?u=6ed3ace2702e5b912b1693cee&id=95823b04c2&e=bf17056ae8&c=1b6625c141) [unsubscribe from this list](https://saikat.us14.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=6ed3ace2702e5b912b1693cee&id=95823b04c2&t=b&e=bf17056ae8&c=1b6625c141) [update subscription preferences](https://saikat.us14.list-manage.com/profile?u=6ed3ace2702e5b912b1693cee&id=95823b04c2&e=bf17056ae8&c=1b6625c141)

# [Republicans Haven't Learned a Damn Thing from Tuesday's Election](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4258115&post_id=178220426&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4MjIwNDI2LCJpYXQiOjE3NjI0Njc4OTQsImV4cCI6MTc2NTA1OTg5NCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQyNTgxMTUiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.bfvHoo-yaKlOe-vCq8vqimfy_g-D4Iry-pPfdD0106s)
### A broken party, clinging to failure and incapable of change.
| | [Mike Nellis](https://substack.com/@mikenellis)
---
| Nov 6
---
| [](https://substack.com/@mikenellis)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4258115&post_id=178220426&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4MjIwNDI2LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2MjQ2Nzg5NCwiZXhwIjoxNzY1MDU5ODk0LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNDI1ODExNSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.1s3Y9JLpRCSHGmoSpV3IqVM76LvnSoaAI6zgNb0GPLY&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4258115&post_id=178220426&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4MjIwNDI2LCJpYXQiOjE3NjI0Njc4OTQsImV4cCI6MTc2NTA1OTg5NCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQyNTgxMTUiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.bfvHoo-yaKlOe-vCq8vqimfy_g-D4Iry-pPfdD0106s)

---

_**[Endless Urgency is free to read, but we're hustling to launch our new content studio and expand our team with a video editor. If you've been enjoying the work, now's the time to back it. Paid subscribers make it possible--just $8/month makes a huge difference: ](https://substack.com/redirect/86adef09-c4a2-44a3-8ac4-e3f7ceb6560f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/86adef09-c4a2-44a3-8ac4-e3f7ceb6560f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Earlier today, I was back on conservative media talking about Tuesday's election results, and I was struck--again--by how the Republican I was debating hasn't pivoted her stance on affordability (or any issue, really), despite the humiliating defeat of her own party this week. It was the same boring-ass talking points, and she wouldn't even acknowledge that the economy is struggling right now.
I've said it before: any healthy political party would look at what happened Tuesday and recognize it as a complete and total repudiation of what the President and the Republican Congress have done over the last 10 months. A normal party would pivot. Tune in and get a pulse on what is actually happening in this country. They'd try to focus on stuff that actually helps people. Try to tell a different story. Try to meet the moment and meet their constituencies where they're at.
But instead? They're doubling down on the same stupid, racist, corrupt bullshit that got them here in the first place.
Instead of delivering real results--stuff that helps people put food on the table, buy a house, send their kids to college, retire with dignity, maybe even take a goddamn vacation--they're digging in even harder.
You see it with the Islamophobic and xenophobic attacks on Zohran Mamdani. The person I debated basically said New York is going to become a socialist Muslim hellhole because of him. She said the left is going to get more violent because Jay Jones was elected Attorney General in Virginia. Give me a break.
Look--I've said before that what Jay Jones said was wrong. He shouldn't have said it. But it damn sure wasn't what Republicans are making it out to be. They've blown it up into something much worse. (And by the way, if Republicans really believe that what Jones said was so wrong, what does it say about what they're doing that he won anyway?)
When Jay Jones faced that leak, he didn't double down or hide behind talking points. He took accountability. He apologized. He said he'd do better--and he followed through. That matters. Because it shows what real values look like in action: not perfection, but the willingness to grow and correct course.
Meanwhile, Republicans only seem to remember their so-called "values" when they can weaponize them. They talk about "law and order" and "respect for America" but stayed silent when one of their own staff flew a defaced American flag--a swastika, a literal emblem of hate. They call themselves "pro-life" and "anti-violence" but said nothing when Melissa Hortman was targeted and killed; Trump didn't even show up to her funeral. They lecture Democrats on morality but can't muster the courage to hold their own accountable.
That's the difference between a party trying to learn and a party too broken to change. Democrats, imperfect as we are, are at least still capable of reflection and renewal. Pelosi stepped aside to give way to a new generation of leadership in her district. Hakeem Jeffries listened to his own constituents and endorsed Zohran Mamdani. Jay Jones owned his mistake, and the voters rewarded him for it. That's what growth looks like. That's what leadership looks like.
_**[Endless Urgency is free to read, but we're hustling to launch our new content studio and expand our team with a video editor. If you've been enjoying the work, now's the time to back it. Paid subscribers make it possible--just $8/month makes a huge difference:](https://substack.com/redirect/86adef09-c4a2-44a3-8ac4-e3f7ceb6560f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/86adef09-c4a2-44a3-8ac4-e3f7ceb6560f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
And then there's Trump. He's suddenly talking more about affordability--but still refuses to acknowledge that his tariffs are making everything more expensive. He was on TV again today, straight-up lying, saying grocery prices are lower now than they were 10 months ago when he took office.
It's a bald-faced lie.
Anyone balancing a household budget, anyone trying to get a job in this market, knows the economy is significantly worse than when Trump took over.
Here's how I've described it: I don't think the Biden economy was as good as Democrats made it out to be last cycle. If it were, we probably would've won in 2024--even with everything else going on.
Trump inherited a relatively contained fire--something we were close to putting out. We were getting back to a place where people could grow their net worth, take care of their families, and build meaningful lives of dignity.
And what did he do? He came in, dumped gasoline on it, and fanned the flames.
Now they've spent the last 10 months gaslighting the American people--telling them their lives are better. Telling them this is a golden age. MAGA influencers, White House staffers, all of them.
It's ridiculous. And frankly, it's insulting.
I've been doing this for two decades. I've worked on campaigns where we won big, and I've worked on campaigns where we got crushed.
And when we got beat in 2024, I didn't bury my head in the sand or cling to the same stale talking points. I paused. Looked at the numbers. Took accountability. And then I tried to help build something better--something that actually resonates with and helps everyday people. That's literally why _Endless Urgency_ exists: because when you lose, you change. You listen. You grow.
And to their credit, the Democratic Party has--imperfectly, but meaningfully--tried to do that work. We're learning. We're getting better.
Republicans? They refuse. They cannot do it.
Why? Because the GOP is no longer a political party. It's a personality cult. A one-man show run by a lame-duck, vengeful wannabe strongman named Donald J. Trump. If you speak out, he'll hand you your pink slip in 2026.
Take Don Bacon from Nebraska. One of the only Republicans talking honestly about inflation. Now he's done. Not running for re-election. That seat is wide open, and Democrats have a real shot to flip it.
And who's the only other Republican calling out Trump on the economy?
Marjorie. Fucking. Taylor. Greene.
Watching her "go woke" has been wild. And yeah, I get it--it's a cynical move. She's mad she couldn't run statewide in Georgia. She wants to run for president instead.
_**[Endless Urgency is free to read, but we're hustling to launch our new content studio and expand our team with a video editor. If you've been enjoying the work, now's the time to back it. Paid subscribers make it possible--just $8/month makes a huge difference:](https://substack.com/redirect/86adef09-c4a2-44a3-8ac4-e3f7ceb6560f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/86adef09-c4a2-44a3-8ac4-e3f7ceb6560f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
But the fact that she _is_ reading the polls, recognizing how bad things are, and trying to reposition ahead of the rest of her party? That tells you everything you need to know.
Because behind closed doors, every Republican knows the truth. They know their constituents are struggling. They know this government shutdown is a disaster--for SNAP recipients, for federal workers, for travelers heading into the holidays.
They know all of it.
But they still won't break ranks. Because if they do, Trump will destroy them.
But you know what's worse? Abandoning the very people who entrusted them to protect their constitutional rights--to act in their best interests, to stand up for their freedoms, and to make the world a better place for their children.
Republicans have been living in fantasyland and have just reaped the consequences of their actions. And still, they've learned nothing.
Case in point: when I was on _NewsNation_ the other night, mid-debate, a White House aide texted one of the anchors and said, "Nothing has changed in the country after this election."
Are you kidding me?
That mindset is exactly why we have to keep fighting.
We should take a beat to celebrate--because winning these elections feels really good. And we should soak that in.
Lib out all you want this week.
But we've got work to do.
Because they're not going to change--not before the midterms, maybe not even after--unless we force through consecutive electoral drubbings.
We have to keep building a better Democratic Party. Keep lifting up new leaders. Keep winning races.
We've got to stay focused on affordability, on security, on corruption. If we do that, we can take back the House. And I _do_ believe we can take back the Senate in 2026.
That gets us one hand back on the wheel.
And then we can stop the tariffs. Stop the ICE raids. Stop the damage. Inject some actual accountability and transparency into this shitshow. And set ourselves up to take back the White House in 2028.
Because at the end of the day, this is what politics should be about: improving people's lives.
With urgency,
--Mike

_Mike Nellis is a Democratic strategist and entrepreneur who has raised over $1 billion for Democratic campaigns and causes. He 's the founder of Authentic.org, an award-winning fundraising and advertising agency, and a former Senior Advisor to Vice President Kamala Harris. clean_body: A broken party, clinging to failure and incapable of change. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? A broken party, clinging to failure and incapable of change. | | | Nov 6 | | | | | | | | | | | | Earlier today, I was back on conservative media talking about Tuesday's election results, and I was struck--again--by how the Republican I was debating hasn't pivoted her stance on affordability (or any issue, really), despite the humiliating defeat of her own party this week. It was the same boring-ass talking points, and she wouldn't even acknowledge that the economy is struggling right now. I've said it before: any healthy political party would look at what happened Tuesday and recognize it as a complete and total repudiation of what the President and the Republican Congress have done over the last 10 months. A normal party would pivot. Tune in and get a pulse on what is actually happening in this country. They'd try to focus on stuff that actually helps people. Try to tell a different story. Try to meet the moment and meet their constituencies where they're at. But instead? They're doubling down on the same stupid, racist, corrupt bullshit that got them here in the first place. Instead of delivering real results--stuff that helps people put food on the table, buy a house, send their kids to college, retire with dignity, maybe even take a goddamn vacation--they're digging in even harder. You see it with the Islamophobic and xenophobic attacks on Zohran Mamdani. The person I debated basically said New York is going to become a socialist Muslim hellhole because of him. She said the left is going to get more violent because Jay Jones was elected Attorney General in Virginia. Give me a break. Look--I've said before that what Jay Jones said was wrong. He shouldn't have said it. But it damn sure wasn't what Republicans are making it out to be. They've blown it up into something much worse. (And by the way, if Republicans really believe that what Jones said was so wrong, what does it say about what they're doing that he won anyway?) When Jay Jones faced that leak, he didn't double down or hide behind talking points. He took accountability. He apologized. He said he'd do better--and he followed through. That matters. Because it shows what real values look like in action: not perfection, but the willingness to grow and correct course. Meanwhile, Republicans only seem to remember their so-called "values" when they can weaponize them. They talk about "law and order" and "respect for America" but stayed silent when one of their own staff flew a defaced American flag--a swastika, a literal emblem of hate. They call themselves "pro-life" and "anti-violence" but said nothing when Melissa Hortman was targeted and killed; Trump didn't even show up to her funeral. They lecture Democrats on morality but can't muster the courage to hold their own accountable. That's the difference between a party trying to learn and a party too broken to change. Democrats, imperfect as we are, are at least still capable of reflection and renewal. Pelosi stepped aside to give way to a new generation of leadership in her district. Hakeem Jeffries listened to his own constituents and endorsed Zohran Mamdani. Jay Jones owned his mistake, and the voters rewarded him for it. That's what growth looks like. That's what leadership looks like. And then there's Trump. He's suddenly talking more about affordability--but still refuses to acknowledge that his tariffs are making everything more expensive. He was on TV again today, straight-up lying, saying grocery prices are lower now than they were 10 months ago when he took office. It's a bald-faced lie. Anyone balancing a household budget, anyone trying to get a job in this market, knows the economy is significantly worse than when Trump took over. Here's how I've described it: I don't think the Biden economy was as good as Democrats made it out to be last cycle. If it were, we probably would've won in 2024--even with everything else going on. Trump inherited a relatively contained fire--something we were close to putting out. We were getting back to a place where people could grow their net worth, take care of their families, and build meaningful lives of dignity. And what did he do? He came in, dumped gasoline on it, and fanned the flames. Now they've spent the last 10 months gaslighting the American people--telling them their lives are better. Telling them this is a golden age. MAGA influencers, White House staffers, all of them. It's ridiculous. And frankly, it's insulting. I've been doing this for two decades. I've worked on campaigns where we won big, and I've worked on campaigns where we got crushed. And when we got beat in 2024, I didn't bury my head in the sand or cling to the same stale talking points. I paused. Looked at the numbers. Took accountability. And then I tried to help build something better--something that actually resonates with and helps everyday people. That's literally why Endless Urgency exists: because when you lose, you change. You listen. You grow. And to their credit, the Democratic Party has--imperfectly, but meaningfully--tried to do that work. We're learning. We're getting better. Republicans? They refuse. They cannot do it. Why? Because the GOP is no longer a political party. It's a personality cult. A one-man show run by a lame-duck, vengeful wannabe strongman named Donald J. Trump. If you speak out, he'll hand you your pink slip in 2026. Take Don Bacon from Nebraska. One of the only Republicans talking honestly about inflation. Now he's done. Not running for re-election. That seat is wide open, and Democrats have a real shot to flip it. And who's the only other Republican calling out Trump on the economy? Marjorie. Fucking. Taylor. Greene. Watching her "go woke" has been wild. And yeah, I get it--it's a cynical move. She's mad she couldn't run statewide in Georgia. She wants to run for president instead. But the fact that she is reading the polls, recognizing how bad things are, and trying to reposition ahead of the rest of her party? That tells you everything you need to know. Because behind closed doors, every Republican knows the truth. They know their constituents are struggling. They know this government shutdown is a disaster--for SNAP recipients, for federal workers, for travelers heading into the holidays. They know all of it. But they still won't break ranks. Because if they do, Trump will destroy them. But you know what's worse? Abandoning the very people who entrusted them to protect their constitutional rights--to act in their best interests, to stand up for their freedoms, and to make the world a better place for their children. Republicans have been living in fantasyland and have just reaped the consequences of their actions. And still, they've learned nothing. Case in point: when I was on NewsNation the other night, mid-debate, a White House aide texted one of the anchors and said, "Nothing has changed in the country after this election." Are you kidding me? That mindset is exactly why we have to keep fighting. We should take a beat to celebrate--because winning these elections feels really good. And we should soak that in. Lib out all you want this week. But we've got work to do. Because they're not going to change--not before the midterms, maybe not even after--unless we force through consecutive electoral drubbings. We have to keep building a better Democratic Party. Keep lifting up new leaders. Keep winning races. We've got to stay focused on affordability, on security, on corruption. If we do that, we can take back the House. And I do believe we can take back the Senate in 2026. That gets us one hand back on the wheel. And then we can stop the tariffs. Stop the ICE raids. Stop the damage. Inject some actual accountability and transparency into this shitshow. And set ourselves up to take back the White House in 2028. Because at the end of the day, this is what politics should be about: improving people's lives. With urgency, --Mike Mike Nellis is a Democratic strategist and entrepreneur who has raised over $1 billion for Democratic campaigns and causes. He 's the founder of Authentic.org, an award-winning fundraising and advertising agency, and a former Senior Advisor to Vice President Kamala Harris. This Substack is where he writes with endless urgency about the issues of the day--and how we can save the Democratic Party and our democracy.
